5190: 
5191:     // Excavate: hex selection for teleport at start of turn
5192:     enterExcavateTeleportMode(playerIndex) {
5193:         const self = this;
5194: 
5195:         updateStatus('Excavate: Click any unoccupied hex on a revealed tile to teleport there.');
5196: 
5197:         this.selectionMode = {
5198:             type: 'excavate-teleport',
5199:             casterIndex: playerIndex,
5200: 
5201:             handleHexClick(hexPos) {
5202:                 const destX = hexPos.x;
5203:                 const destY = hexPos.y;
5204: 
5205:                 // Check hex is on at least one revealed (non-player) tile
5206:                 const hasRevealedTile = hexPos.tiles && hexPos.tiles.some(t => !t.flipped && !t.isPlayerTile);
5207:                 if (!hasRevealedTile) {
5208:                     updateStatus('Cannot teleport there - not on a revealed tile!');
5209:                     return;
5210:                 }
5211: 
5212:                 // Check not occupied by a stone
5213:                 const hasStone = (typeof placedStones !== 'undefined') && placedStones.some(s => {
5214:                     const dist = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(s.x - destX, 2) + Math.pow(s.y - destY, 2));
5215:                     return dist < 5;
5216:                 });
5217:                 if (hasStone) {
5218:                     updateStatus('Cannot teleport there - a stone is on that space!');
5219:                     return;
5220:                 }
5221: 
5222:                 // Check not occupied by a player
5223:                 const hasPlayer = (typeof playerPositions !== 'undefined') && playerPositions.some(p => {
5224:                     if (!p) return false;
5225:                     const dist = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(p.x - destX, 2) + Math.pow(p.y - destY, 2));
5226:                     return dist < 5;
5227:                 });
5228:                 if (hasPlayer) {
5229:                     updateStatus('Cannot teleport there - a player is on that space!');
5230:                     return;
5231:                 }
5232: 
5233:                 // Teleport the player
5234:                 if (typeof placePlayer === 'function') {
5235:                     placePlayer(destX, destY);
5236:                 }
5237: 
5238:                 updateStatus(`Excavate: Teleported! Your turn begins.`);
5239:                 console.log(`⛏️ Excavate teleport: player ${playerIndex} to (${destX.toFixed(1)}, ${destY.toFixed(1)})`);
5240: 
5241:                 // Broadcast in multiplayer
5242:                 if (typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer && typeof broadcastGameAction === 'function') {
5243:                     broadcastGameAction('excavate-teleport', {
5244:                         playerIndex: playerIndex,
5245:                         x: destX,
5246:                         y: destY
5247:                     });
5248:                 }
5249: 
5250:                 if (typeof syncPlayerState === 'function') syncPlayerState();
5251: 
5252:                 self.selectionMode = null;
5253:             },
5254: 
5255:             cleanup() {
5256:                 // No cancel button to clean up
5257:             }
5258:         };
5259:     }
5260: };
5261: 
5262: // Export for use
5263: if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
5264:     window.ScrollEffects = ScrollEffects;
5265: }
</file>

<file path="js/scrolls/response-window.js">
   1: /**
   2:  * Response Window System for Godaigo
   3:  *
   4:  * When a player casts a scroll, a "response window" opens allowing ONE other player
   5:  * to respond with their own scroll.
   6:  *
   7:  * Rules:
   8:  * - Response costs normal 2 AP
   9:  * - Responder must have valid scroll in active area AND valid pattern on board
  10:  * - Earth I (Iron Stance) can counter any scroll
  11:  * - Response window can be skipped if no player has a valid response
  12:  * - Only ONE response is allowed, then the stack resolves immediately
  13:  */
  14: 
  15: class ResponseWindowSystem {
  16:     constructor(spellSystem) {
  17:         this.spellSystem = spellSystem;
  18:         this.isResponseWindowOpen = false;
  19:         this.responseStack = []; // Stack of pending scroll casts
  20:         this.currentCaster = null; // Player who initiated the current scroll
  21:         this.pendingScrollData = null; // Data about the scroll being responded to
  22:         this.respondingPlayers = new Set(); // Players who have passed or responded
  23:         this.responseTimeout = null;
  24:         this.RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000; // 15 seconds to respond
  25:         this.responseModalElement = null;
  26:     }
  27: 
  28:     /**
  29:      * Check if a player can respond to a scroll
  30:      * @param {number} playerIndex - The player to check
  31:      * @returns {object} { canRespond: boolean, validScrolls: array, reason: string }
  32:      */
  33:     canPlayerRespond(playerIndex) {
  34:         // Determine player's available AP (responses may be free with buffs)
  35:         const playerAP = this.getPlayerAP(playerIndex);
  36: 
  37:         // Get player's active scrolls and common area scrolls
  38:         const playerScrolls = this.spellSystem.playerScrolls[playerIndex];
  39:         if (!playerScrolls) {
  40:             return { canRespond: false, validScrolls: [], reason: 'No scrolls available' };
  41:         }
  42: 
  43:         const activeScrolls = Array.from(playerScrolls.active || []);
  44:         const commonScrolls = this.spellSystem.getCommonAreaScrolls();
  45:         const allCastableScrolls = [...activeScrolls, ...commonScrolls];
  46: 
  47:         if (allCastableScrolls.length === 0) {
  48:             return { canRespond: false, validScrolls: [], reason: 'No scrolls in active area or common area' };
  49:         }
  50: 
  51:         // Check which scrolls have valid patterns on the board
  52:         const validScrolls = [];
  53:         const playerPos = this.getPlayerPosition(playerIndex);
  54: 
  55:         if (!playerPos) {
  56:             return { canRespond: false, validScrolls: [], reason: 'Player position not found' };
  57:         }
  58: 
  59:         for (const scrollName of allCastableScrolls) {
  60:             const patternOk = this.checkPatternForPlayer(scrollName, playerIndex);
  61:             if (patternOk) {
  62:                 const scrollDef = this.spellSystem.patterns[scrollName];
  63:                 const isCounter = scrollDef?.canCounter === 'any';
  64:                 const isResponse = scrollDef?.isResponse === true;
  65: 
  66:                 // Only include scrolls that are counter scrolls OR response scrolls
  67:                 // Regular scrolls can't be cast as responses
  68:                 if (isCounter || isResponse) {
  69:                     const cost = this.spellSystem?.getSpellCost ? this.spellSystem.getSpellCost(scrollDef, playerIndex) : 2;
  70:                     if (playerAP < cost) {
  71:                         console.log(`  ↳ ${scrollName}: pattern OK, isResponse=${isResponse} but can't afford (AP=${playerAP}, cost=${cost})`);
  72:                         continue;
  73:                     }
  74:                     validScrolls.push({
  75:                         name: scrollName,
  76:                         definition: scrollDef,
  77:                         isCounter: isCounter,
  78:                         isResponse: isResponse,
  79:                         fromCommonArea: commonScrolls.includes(scrollName),
  80:                         cost: cost
  81:                     });
  82:                 } else {
  83:                     console.log(`  ↳ ${scrollName}: pattern OK but not a counter/response scroll`);
  84:                 }
  85:             } else {
  86:                 console.log(`  ↳ ${scrollName}: pattern NOT matched for player ${playerIndex}`);
  87:             }
  88:         }
  89: 
  90:         if (validScrolls.length === 0) {
  91:             return { canRespond: false, validScrolls: [], reason: `No valid responses you can afford (AP=${playerAP})` };
  92:         }
  93: 
  94:         return { canRespond: true, validScrolls, reason: null };
  95:     }
  96: 
  97:     /**
  98:      * Check if any player can respond (used to skip response window)
  99:      * @param {number} excludePlayer - Player to exclude from check (the caster cannot respond to their own scroll)
 100:      * @returns {boolean}
 101:      */
 102:     canAnyPlayerRespond(excludePlayer = -1) {
 103:         const numPlayers = typeof playerPositions !== 'undefined' ? playerPositions.length : 0;
 104:         console.log(`Response window: checking ${numPlayers} players for valid responses (excluding player ${excludePlayer})`);
 105: 
 106:         for (let i = 0; i < numPlayers; i++) {
 107:             // Skip the caster - you cannot respond to your own scroll
 108:             if (i === excludePlayer) {
 109:                 console.log(`  Player ${i}: skipped (caster)`);
 110:                 continue;
 111:             }
 112:             const result = this.canPlayerRespond(i);
 113:             console.log(`  Player ${i}: canRespond=${result.canRespond}, reason=${result.reason || 'can respond'}, validScrolls=${result.validScrolls.length}`);
 114:             if (result.canRespond) {
 115:                 return true;
 116:             }
 117:         }
 118:         return false;
 119:     }
 120: 
 121:     /**
 122:      * Open response window after a scroll is cast
 123:      * @param {object} scrollData - Data about the cast scroll
 124:      * @param {number} casterIndex - Index of the player who cast the scroll
 125:      * @param {function} onComplete - Callback when response window closes
 126:      */
 127:     openResponseWindow(scrollData, casterIndex, onComplete) {
 128:         const numPlayers = typeof playerPositions !== 'undefined' ? playerPositions.length : 0;
 129:         const hasOtherPlayer = numPlayers > 1;
 130:         if (!hasOtherPlayer) {
 131:             console.log('Response window skipped - only one player');
 132:             if (onComplete) onComplete({ skipped: true, responses: [] });
 133:             return;
 134:         }
 135: 
 136:         // Quick check: can any non-caster player actually respond?
 137:         // In SINGLE-PLAYER we skip the response window when no one can respond.
 138:         // In MULTIPLAYER we do NOT skip here — this check runs on the caster's client
 139:         // using its local snapshot of opponent scroll state, which can lag behind due
 140:         // to in-flight `scroll-move` broadcasts. Skipping would block valid Iron Stance
 141:         // counters that arrived just before the winning scroll was cast.
 142:         // Each non-caster client already checks their own state locally inside
 143:         // showResponseModalForOtherPlayer() and auto-passes instantly if they can't
 144:         // respond, so no one is ever stuck waiting.
 145:         const _inMultiplayer = typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer;
 146:         if (!_inMultiplayer && !this.canAnyPlayerRespond(casterIndex)) {
 147:             console.log('Response window skipped - no player can respond (no valid counter/response scrolls)');
 148:             if (onComplete) onComplete({ skipped: true, responses: [] });
 149:             return;
 150:         }
 151: 
 152:         this.isResponseWindowOpen = true;
 153:         this.currentCaster = casterIndex;
 154:         this.pendingScrollData = scrollData;
 155:         this.respondingPlayers.clear();
 156:         // The caster counts as already having "responded" (they cast the spell)
 157:         this.respondingPlayers.add(casterIndex);
 158:         this.responseStack = [{
 159:             scrollData,
 160:             casterIndex,
 161:             isOriginal: true
 162:         }];
 163: 
 164:         // Store callback
 165:         this.onCompleteCallback = onComplete;
 166: 
 167:         // Broadcast in multiplayer FIRST so other players see the window
 168:         if (typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer) {
 169:             this.broadcastResponseWindowOpened(scrollData, casterIndex);
 170:         }
 171: 
 172:         // For the caster, show "waiting for responses" instead of the response modal
 173:         const myIndex = typeof myPlayerIndex !== 'undefined' ? myPlayerIndex : activePlayerIndex;
 174:         if (myIndex === casterIndex) {
 175:             // Caster waits for others
 176:             this.showWaitingForResponses(scrollData);
 177:         } else {
 178:             // Non-caster sees the response modal
 179:             this.showResponseModal();
 180:         }
 181: 
 182:         // Start timeout timer
 183:         this.startResponseTimeout();
 184:     }
 185: 
 186:     /**
 187:      * Show the response window modal overlay
 188:      */
 189:     showResponseModal() {
 190:         // Remove existing modal if present
 191:         if (this.responseModalElement) {
 192:             this.responseModalElement.remove();
 193:         }
 194: 
 195:         // Create overlay backdrop
 196:         const overlay = document.createElement('div');
 197:         overlay.id = 'response-window-overlay';
 198:         Object.assign(overlay.style, {
 199:             position: 'fixed',
 200:             top: '0',
 201:             left: '0',
 202:             right: '0',
 203:             bottom: '0',
 204:             backgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.7)',
 205:             zIndex: '2000',
 206:             display: 'flex',
 207:             justifyContent: 'center',
 208:             alignItems: 'center'
 209:         });
 210: 
 211:         // Create modal
 212:         const modal = document.createElement('div');
 213:         modal.id = 'response-window-modal';
 214:         Object.assign(modal.style, {
 215:             backgroundColor: '#1a1a2e',
 216:             border: '3px solid #e94560',
 217:             borderRadius: '15px',
 218:             padding: '25px',
 219:             minWidth: '450px',
 220:             maxWidth: '600px',
 221:             maxHeight: '80vh',
 222:             overflowY: 'auto',
 223:             color: 'white',
 224:             boxShadow: '0 0 30px rgba(233, 69, 96, 0.5)'
 225:         });
 226: 
 227:         // Header
 228:         const header = document.createElement('div');
 229:         header.style.textAlign = 'center';
 230:         header.style.marginBottom = '20px';
 231: 
 232:         const title = document.createElement('h2');
 233:         title.textContent = 'RESPONSE WINDOW';
 234:         title.style.color = '#e94560';
 235:         title.style.margin = '0 0 10px 0';
 236:         title.style.textTransform = 'uppercase';
 237:         title.style.letterSpacing = '3px';
 238:         header.appendChild(title);
 239: 
 240:         // Show what scroll was cast
 241:         const scrollInfo = document.createElement('div');
 242:         const scrollDef = this.spellSystem.patterns[this.pendingScrollData.name];
 243:         scrollInfo.innerHTML = `<strong>${this.getPlayerName(this.currentCaster)}</strong> cast <span style="color: ${this.getElementColor(scrollDef?.element)}">${scrollDef?.name || this.pendingScrollData.name}</span>`;
 244:         scrollInfo.style.fontSize = '16px';
 245:         scrollInfo.style.marginBottom = '10px';
 246:         header.appendChild(scrollInfo);
 247: 
 248:         // Timer display
 249:         const timerDiv = document.createElement('div');
 250:         timerDiv.id = 'response-timer';
 251:         timerDiv.style.fontSize = '24px';
 252:         timerDiv.style.fontWeight = 'bold';
 253:         timerDiv.style.color = '#f39c12';
 254:         timerDiv.textContent = `${Math.ceil(this.RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_MS / 1000)}s`;
 255:         header.appendChild(timerDiv);
 256: 
 257:         modal.appendChild(header);
 258: 
 259:         // Check if current player can respond
 260:         const myIndex = typeof myPlayerIndex !== 'undefined' ? myPlayerIndex : activePlayerIndex;
 261:         const responseCheck = this.canPlayerRespond(myIndex);
 262: 
 263:         if (responseCheck.canRespond) {
 264:             // Show available response scrolls
 265:             const scrollsSection = document.createElement('div');
 266:             scrollsSection.style.marginBottom = '20px';
 267: 
 268:             const scrollsHeader = document.createElement('h3');
 269:             scrollsHeader.textContent = 'Your Available Responses:';
 270:             scrollsHeader.style.color = '#16a085';
 271:             scrollsHeader.style.marginBottom = '15px';
 272:             scrollsSection.appendChild(scrollsHeader);
 273: 
 274:             responseCheck.validScrolls.forEach(scrollInfo => {
 275:                 const scrollCard = this.createScrollCard(scrollInfo);
 276:                 scrollsSection.appendChild(scrollCard);
 277:             });
 278: 
 279:             modal.appendChild(scrollsSection);
 280:         } else {
 281:             // Show why player can't respond
 282:             const noResponseDiv = document.createElement('div');
 283:             noResponseDiv.style.textAlign = 'center';
 284:             noResponseDiv.style.padding = '20px';
 285:             noResponseDiv.style.color = '#95a5a6';
 286:             noResponseDiv.innerHTML = `<p>You cannot respond:</p><p><em>${responseCheck.reason}</em></p>`;
 287:             modal.appendChild(noResponseDiv);
 288:         }
 289: 
 290:         // Pass button
 291:         const buttonContainer = document.createElement('div');
 292:         buttonContainer.style.display = 'flex';
 293:         buttonContainer.style.justifyContent = 'center';
 294:         buttonContainer.style.gap = '15px';
 295:         buttonContainer.style.marginTop = '20px';
 296: 
 297:         const passBtn = document.createElement('button');
 298:         passBtn.textContent = 'Pass (No Response)';
 299:         Object.assign(passBtn.style, {
 300:             padding: '12px 30px',
 301:             fontSize: '16px',
 302:             backgroundColor: '#7f8c8d',
 303:             color: 'white',
 304:             border: 'none',
 305:             borderRadius: '8px',
 306:             cursor: 'pointer',
 307:             fontWeight: 'bold'
 308:         });
 309:         passBtn.onmouseenter = () => passBtn.style.backgroundColor = '#95a5a6';
 310:         passBtn.onmouseleave = () => passBtn.style.backgroundColor = '#7f8c8d';
 311:         passBtn.onclick = () => this.playerPasses(myIndex);
 312:         buttonContainer.appendChild(passBtn);
 313: 
 314:         modal.appendChild(buttonContainer);
 315: 
 316:         overlay.appendChild(modal);
 317:         document.body.appendChild(overlay);
 318: 
 319:         this.responseModalElement = overlay;
 320:     }
 321: 
 322:     /**
 323:      * Create a scroll card for the response modal
 324:      */
 325:     createScrollCard(scrollInfo) {
 326:         const card = document.createElement('div');
 327:         const elementColor = this.getElementColor(scrollInfo.definition?.element);
 328: 
 329:         // Determine border color based on scroll type
 330:         let borderColor = elementColor;
 331:         if (scrollInfo.isCounter) {
 332:             borderColor = '#e74c3c'; // Red for counter
 333:         } else if (scrollInfo.isResponse) {
 334:             borderColor = '#f39c12'; // Orange for response
 335:         }
 336: 
 337:         Object.assign(card.style, {
 338:             backgroundColor: '#2d2d44',
 339:             border: '2px solid ' + borderColor,
 340:             borderRadius: '10px',
 341:             padding: '15px',
 342:             marginBottom: '10px',
 343:             cursor: 'pointer',
 344:             transition: 'all 0.2s'
 345:         });
 346: 
 347:         card.onmouseenter = () => {
 348:             card.style.backgroundColor = '#3d3d54';
 349:             card.style.transform = 'scale(1.02)';
 350:         };
 351:         card.onmouseleave = () => {
 352:             card.style.backgroundColor = '#2d2d44';
 353:             card.style.transform = 'scale(1)';
 354:         };
 355: 
 356:         // Header
 357:         const headerDiv = document.createElement('div');
 358:         headerDiv.style.display = 'flex';
 359:         headerDiv.style.justifyContent = 'space-between';
 360:         headerDiv.style.alignItems = 'center';
 361: 
 362:         const nameSpan = document.createElement('span');
 363:         nameSpan.textContent = scrollInfo.definition?.name || scrollInfo.name;
 364:         nameSpan.style.fontWeight = 'bold';
 365:         nameSpan.style.color = elementColor;
 366:         nameSpan.style.fontSize = '16px';
 367:         headerDiv.appendChild(nameSpan);
 368: 
 369:         if (scrollInfo.isCounter) {
 370:             const counterBadge = document.createElement('span');
 371:             counterBadge.textContent = 'COUNTER';
 372:             Object.assign(counterBadge.style, {
 373:                 backgroundColor: '#e74c3c',
 374:                 color: 'white',
 375:                 padding: '3px 8px',
 376:                 borderRadius: '4px',
 377:                 fontSize: '10px',
 378:                 fontWeight: 'bold'
 379:             });
 380:             headerDiv.appendChild(counterBadge);
 381:         } else if (scrollInfo.isResponse) {
 382:             const responseBadge = document.createElement('span');
 383:             responseBadge.textContent = 'RESPONSE';
 384:             Object.assign(responseBadge.style, {
 385:                 backgroundColor: '#f39c12',
 386:                 color: 'white',
 387:                 padding: '3px 8px',
 388:                 borderRadius: '4px',
 389:                 fontSize: '10px',
 390:                 fontWeight: 'bold'
 391:             });
 392:             headerDiv.appendChild(responseBadge);
 393:         }
 394: 
 395:         if (scrollInfo.fromCommonArea) {
 396:             const commonBadge = document.createElement('span');
 397:             commonBadge.textContent = 'COMMON';
 398:             Object.assign(commonBadge.style, {
 399:                 backgroundColor: '#9b59b6',
 400:                 color: 'white',
 401:                 padding: '3px 8px',
 402:                 borderRadius: '4px',
 403:                 fontSize: '10px',
 404:                 fontWeight: 'bold',
 405:                 marginLeft: '5px'
 406:             });
 407:             headerDiv.appendChild(commonBadge);
 408:         }
 409: 
 410:         card.appendChild(headerDiv);
 411: 
 412:         // Description
 413:         const descDiv = document.createElement('div');
 414:         descDiv.textContent = scrollInfo.definition?.description || '';
 415:         descDiv.style.fontSize = '12px';
 416:         descDiv.style.color = '#bdc3c7';
 417:         descDiv.style.marginTop = '8px';
 418:         card.appendChild(descDiv);
 419: 
 420:         // Cost info
 421:         const costDiv = document.createElement('div');
 422:         const displayCost = (scrollInfo && typeof scrollInfo.cost === 'number') ? scrollInfo.cost : 2;
 423:         costDiv.textContent = `${displayCost} AP`;
 424:         costDiv.style.fontSize = '12px';
 425:         costDiv.style.color = '#f39c12';
 426:         costDiv.style.marginTop = '5px';
 427:         card.appendChild(costDiv);
 428: 
 429:         // Click to respond
 430:         card.onclick = () => this.playerResponds(scrollInfo);
 431: 
 432:         return card;
 433:     }
 434: 
 435:     /**
 436:      * Handle player choosing to respond with a scroll
 437:      * NOTE: Only ONE response is allowed per scroll cast - no stack continuation
 438:      */
 439:     playerResponds(scrollInfo) {
 440:         const myIndex = typeof myPlayerIndex !== 'undefined' ? myPlayerIndex : activePlayerIndex;
 441:         console.log(`playerResponds called: myIndex=${myIndex}, scroll=${scrollInfo.name}`);
 442: 
 443:         // Double check they can still afford it
 444:         const myAP = this.getPlayerAP(myIndex);
 445:         const cost = (scrollInfo && typeof scrollInfo.cost === 'number')
 446:             ? scrollInfo.cost
 447:             : (this.spellSystem?.getSpellCost ? this.spellSystem.getSpellCost(scrollInfo.definition, myIndex) : 2);
 448:         console.log(`  Player AP: ${myAP}, cost=${cost}`);
 449:         if (myAP < cost) {
 450:             this.showResponseError(`Not enough AP! Need ${cost}, have ${myAP}`);
 451:             return;
 452:         }
 453: 
 454:         // Spend the AP for the response
 455:         this.spendPlayerAP(myIndex, cost);
 456:         console.log(`  Spent ${cost} AP for response`);
 457: 
 458:         // Add response to stack
 459:         this.responseStack.push({
 460:             scrollData: {
 461:                 name: scrollInfo.name,
 462:                 definition: scrollInfo.definition,
 463:                 fromCommonArea: scrollInfo.fromCommonArea || false
 464:             },
 465:             casterIndex: myIndex,
 466:             isCounter: scrollInfo.isCounter,
 467:             isResponse: scrollInfo.isResponse,
 468:             fromCommonArea: scrollInfo.fromCommonArea || false,
 469:             isOriginal: false
 470:         });
 471:         console.log(`  Added to response stack, stack size: ${this.responseStack.length}`);
 472: 
 473:         // Broadcast response in multiplayer
 474:         if (typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer) {
 475:             this.broadcastResponse(scrollInfo, myIndex);
 476:             console.log(`  Broadcasted response`);
 477:         }
 478: 
 479:         // Close the current modal
 480:         this.closeResponseModal();
 481: 
 482:         const isCasterClient = !isMultiplayer || myIndex === this.currentCaster;
 483:         if (isCasterClient) {
 484:             // Only ONE response allowed - immediately resolve the stack
 485:             console.log(`  One response received (caster client), resolving stack immediately`);
 486:             this.resolveResponseStack();
 487:         } else {
 488:             // Non-caster: we sent our response, now wait for the caster
 489:             // to receive it, resolve, and broadcast 'response-resolved'
 490:             console.log(`  Response sent, waiting for caster to resolve`);
 491:             this.clearResponseTimeout();
 492:         }
 493:     }
 494: 
 495:     /**
 496:      * Handle player passing (no response)
 497:      */
 498:     playerPasses(playerIndex) {
 499:         this.respondingPlayers.add(playerIndex);
 500: 
 501:         // Broadcast pass in multiplayer
 502:         if (typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer) {
 503:             this.broadcastPass(playerIndex);
 504:         }
 505: 
 506:         const myIndex = typeof myPlayerIndex !== 'undefined' ? myPlayerIndex : null;
 507:         const isCasterClient = !isMultiplayer || myIndex === this.currentCaster;
 508: 
 509:         if (isCasterClient) {
 510:             // Caster's client: check if all others responded, then resolve
 511:             const numPlayers = typeof playerPositions !== 'undefined' ? playerPositions.length : 1;
 512:             if (this.respondingPlayers.size >= numPlayers) {
 513:                 this.resolveResponseStack();
 514:             } else {
 515:                 this.showWaitingForOthers();
 516:             }
 517:         } else {
 518:             // Non-caster's client: just close the modal and wait for
 519:             // the caster to resolve and broadcast 'response-resolved'
 520:             this.closeResponseModal();
 521:             this.clearResponseTimeout();
 522:         }
 523:     }
 524: 
 525:     /**
 526:      * Show waiting message while other players decide
 527:      */
 528:     showWaitingForOthers() {
 529:         const modal = document.getElementById('response-window-modal');
 530:         if (!modal) return;
 531: 
 532:         // Update modal content
 533:         modal.innerHTML = '';
 534: 
 535:         const waitDiv = document.createElement('div');
 536:         waitDiv.style.textAlign = 'center';
 537:         waitDiv.style.padding = '40px';
 538: 
 539:         const spinner = document.createElement('div');
 540:         spinner.innerHTML = '&#x21bb;'; // Circular arrow
 541:         spinner.style.fontSize = '48px';
 542:         spinner.style.animation = 'spin 1s linear infinite';
 543:         waitDiv.appendChild(spinner);
 544: 
 545:         const text = document.createElement('p');
 546:         text.textContent = 'Waiting for other players...';
 547:         text.style.fontSize = '18px';
 548:         text.style.marginTop = '20px';
 549:         waitDiv.appendChild(text);
 550: 
 551:         modal.appendChild(waitDiv);
 552: 
 553:         // Add spin animation if not already present
 554:         if (!document.getElementById('response-spin-style')) {
 555:             const style = document.createElement('style');
 556:             style.id = 'response-spin-style';
 557:             style.textContent = '@keyframes spin { from { transform: rotate(0deg); } to { transform: rotate(360deg); } }';
 558:             document.head.appendChild(style);
 559:         }
 560:     }
 561: 
 562:     /**
 563:      * Show waiting screen for the caster while others respond
 564:      */
 565:     showWaitingForResponses(scrollData) {
 566:         // Remove existing modal if present
 567:         if (this.responseModalElement) {
 568:             this.responseModalElement.remove();
 569:         }
 570: 
 571:         // Create overlay backdrop
 572:         const overlay = document.createElement('div');
 573:         overlay.id = 'response-window-overlay';
 574:         Object.assign(overlay.style, {
 575:             position: 'fixed',
 576:             top: '0',
 577:             left: '0',
 578:             right: '0',
 579:             bottom: '0',
 580:             backgroundColor: 'rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.7)',
 581:             zIndex: '2000',
 582:             display: 'flex',
 583:             justifyContent: 'center',
 584:             alignItems: 'center'
 585:         });
 586: 
 587:         // Create modal
 588:         const modal = document.createElement('div');
 589:         modal.id = 'response-window-modal';
 590:         Object.assign(modal.style, {
 591:             backgroundColor: '#1a1a2e',
 592:             border: '3px solid #27ae60',
 593:             borderRadius: '15px',
 594:             padding: '25px',
 595:             minWidth: '400px',
 596:             maxWidth: '500px',
 597:             color: 'white',
 598:             textAlign: 'center',
 599:             boxShadow: '0 0 30px rgba(39, 174, 96, 0.5)'
 600:         });
 601: 
 602:         const title = document.createElement('h2');
 603:         title.textContent = 'SCROLL CAST';
 604:         title.style.color = '#27ae60';
 605:         title.style.margin = '0 0 15px 0';
 606:         modal.appendChild(title);
 607: 
 608:         const scrollName = document.createElement('div');
 609:         const scrollDef = this.spellSystem?.patterns?.[scrollData.name];
 610:         scrollName.textContent = scrollDef?.name || scrollData.name;
 611:         scrollName.style.fontSize = '20px';
 612:         scrollName.style.fontWeight = 'bold';
 613:         scrollName.style.color = this.getElementColor(scrollDef?.element);
 614:         scrollName.style.marginBottom = '20px';
 615:         modal.appendChild(scrollName);
 616: 
 617:         const waitDiv = document.createElement('div');
 618:         waitDiv.style.padding = '20px';
 619: 
 620:         const spinner = document.createElement('div');
 621:         spinner.innerHTML = '&#x21bb;';
 622:         spinner.style.fontSize = '36px';
 623:         spinner.style.animation = 'spin 1s linear infinite';
 624:         waitDiv.appendChild(spinner);
 625: 
 626:         const text = document.createElement('p');
 627:         text.textContent = 'Waiting for other players to respond...';
 628:         text.style.fontSize = '16px';
 629:         text.style.marginTop = '15px';
 630:         text.style.color = '#bdc3c7';
 631:         waitDiv.appendChild(text);
 632: 
 633:         modal.appendChild(waitDiv);
 634: 
 635:         // Timer display
 636:         const timerDiv = document.createElement('div');
 637:         timerDiv.id = 'response-timer';
 638:         timerDiv.style.fontSize = '20px';
 639:         timerDiv.style.fontWeight = 'bold';
 640:         timerDiv.style.color = '#f39c12';
 641:         timerDiv.style.marginTop = '10px';
 642:         timerDiv.textContent = `${Math.ceil(this.RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_MS / 1000)}s`;
 643:         modal.appendChild(timerDiv);
 644: 
 645:         overlay.appendChild(modal);
 646:         document.body.appendChild(overlay);
 647:         this.responseModalElement = overlay;
 648: 
 649:         // Add spin animation if not already present
 650:         if (!document.getElementById('response-spin-style')) {
 651:             const style = document.createElement('style');
 652:             style.id = 'response-spin-style';
 653:             style.textContent = '@keyframes spin { from { transform: rotate(0deg); } to { transform: rotate(360deg); } }';
 654:             document.head.appendChild(style);
 655:         }
 656:     }
 657: 
 658:     /**
 659:      * Resolve the response stack (LIFO order)
 660:      * FIFO for resolution: responses resolve first, then original scroll
 661:      */
 662:     resolveResponseStack() {
 663:         this.clearResponseTimeout();
 664:         this.closeResponseModal();
 665: 
 666:         // Process stack - responses first, then original
 667:         const results = [];
 668:         let cancelled = false;
 669:         let originalScroll = null;
 670: 
 671:         // Separate responses from original
 672:         const responses = [];
 673:         while (this.responseStack.length > 0) {
 674:             const entry = this.responseStack.pop();
 675:             if (entry.isOriginal) {
 676:                 originalScroll = entry;
 677:             } else {
 678:                 responses.push(entry);
 679:             }
 680:         }
 681: 
 682:         // Process responses first (FIFO - they were added in order)
 683:         for (const entry of responses) {
 684:             const scrollDef = entry.scrollData?.definition || this.spellSystem?.patterns?.[entry.scrollData?.name];
 685: 
 686:             if (entry.isCounter) {
 687:                 // This is a counter - it cancels the original scroll
 688:                 cancelled = true;
 689:                 const counterEntry = {
 690:                     ...entry,
 691:                     result: 'countered-original'
 692:                 };
 693:                 results.push(counterEntry);
 694: 
 695:                 // AP was already spent when the counter was played (line ~439)
 696:                 // Do NOT spend again here — spendPlayerAP ignores playerIndex
 697:                 // and would charge the original caster's AP instead
 698: 
 699:                 if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.logScrollEvent) {
 700:                     window.logScrollEvent('response_counter', {
 701:                         casterIndex: entry.casterIndex,
 702:                         scrollName: entry.scrollData?.name,
 703:                         triggeringScroll: originalScroll?.scrollData?.name || null
 704:                     });
 705:                 }
 706: 
 707:                 // Execute the counter's effect (if any) — must pass counterEntry so
 708:                 // the scroll-resolved listener sees result === 'countered-original'
 709:                 this.executeScrollEffect(counterEntry, originalScroll);
 710:             } else if (entry.isResponse || scrollDef?.isResponse) {
 711:                 // This is a response scroll (like Unbidden Lamplight)
 712:                 // It executes but does NOT cancel the original
 713:                 const resolvedEntry = {
 714:                     ...entry,
 715:                     result: 'response-resolved'
 716:                 };
 717:                 results.push(resolvedEntry);
 718: 
 719:                 // AP was already spent when the response was played (line ~439)
 720:                 // Do NOT spend again here — spendPlayerAP ignores playerIndex
 721:                 // and would charge the original caster's AP instead
 722: 
 723:                 if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.logScrollEvent) {
 724:                     window.logScrollEvent('response_resolved', {
 725:                         casterIndex: entry.casterIndex,
 726:                         scrollName: entry.scrollData?.name,
 727:                         triggeringScroll: originalScroll?.scrollData?.name || null
 728:                     });
 729:                 }
 730: 
 731:                 // Execute the response scroll's effect, passing the original scroll info
 732:                 this.executeScrollEffect(resolvedEntry, originalScroll);
 733:             }
 734:         }
 735: 
 736:         // Now resolve the original scroll (if not cancelled)
 737:         if (originalScroll) {
 738:             if (cancelled) {
 739:                 if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.logScrollEvent) {
 740:                     window.logScrollEvent('original_countered', {
 741:                         casterIndex: originalScroll.casterIndex,
 742:                         scrollName: originalScroll.scrollData?.name
 743:                     });
 744:                 }
 745:                 results.push({
 746:                     ...originalScroll,
 747:                     result: 'countered'
 748:                 });
 749:             } else {
 750:                 if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.logScrollEvent) {
 751:                     window.logScrollEvent('original_resolved', {
 752:                         casterIndex: originalScroll.casterIndex,
 753:                         scrollName: originalScroll.scrollData?.name
 754:                     });
 755:                 }
 756:                 const originalResolvedEntry = { ...originalScroll, result: 'resolved' };
 757:                 results.push(originalResolvedEntry);
 758:                 this.executeScrollEffect(originalResolvedEntry, null);
 759:             }
 760:         }
 761: 
 762:         // Close response window state
 763:         this.isResponseWindowOpen = false;
 764:         this.pendingScrollData = null;
 765:         this.currentCaster = null;
 766: 
 767:         // Broadcast resolution to all clients so they close their windows
 768:         if (typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer) {
 769:             this.broadcastResponseResolved(results, originalScroll);
 770:         }
 771: 
 772:         // Call completion callback
 773:         if (this.onCompleteCallback) {
 774:             this.onCompleteCallback({
 775:                 skipped: false,
 776:                 responses: results
 777:             });
 778:         }
 779:     }
 780: 
 781:     /**
 782:      * Execute a scroll's effect
 783:      * @param {object} entry - The scroll entry to execute
 784:      * @param {object} triggeringScroll - For response scrolls, the original scroll that triggered this response
 785:      */
 786:     executeScrollEffect(entry, triggeringScroll = null) {
 787:         // This delegates back to the main spell system
 788:         // The actual effect execution is handled there
 789:         if (this.spellSystem && typeof this.spellSystem.executeSpell === 'function') {
 790:             // Note: We don't call executeSpell directly here because that would
 791:             // open another response window. Instead, we'll emit an event that
 792:             // the main game loop handles to apply effects without re-triggering responses.
 793:             if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
 794:                 // Resolve the scroll definition from scrollData: it may be stored
 795:                 // as .definition OR .spell depending on the source.
 796:                 const trigDef = triggeringScroll
 797:                     ? (triggeringScroll.scrollData?.definition
 798:                        || triggeringScroll.scrollData?.spell
 799:                        || this.spellSystem.patterns?.[triggeringScroll.scrollData?.name])
 800:                     : null;
 801: 
 802:                 window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('scroll-resolved', {
 803:                     detail: {
 804:                         ...entry,
 805:                         // Promote fromCommonArea to top-level so the listener can access it directly
 806:                         fromCommonArea: entry.fromCommonArea ?? entry.scrollData?.fromCommonArea ?? false,
 807:                         triggeringScroll: triggeringScroll ? {
 808:                             name: triggeringScroll.scrollData?.name,
 809:                             casterIndex: triggeringScroll.casterIndex,
 810:                             definition: trigDef
 811:                         } : null
 812:                     }
 813:                 }));
 814:             }
 815:         }
 816:     }
 817: 
 818:     /**
 819:      * Close the response modal
 820:      */
 821:     closeResponseModal() {
 822:         if (this.responseModalElement) {
 823:             this.responseModalElement.remove();
 824:             this.responseModalElement = null;
 825:         }
 826:     }
 827: 
 828:     /**
 829:      * Start the response timeout timer
 830:      */
 831:     startResponseTimeout() {
 832:         this.clearResponseTimeout();
 833: 
 834:         let remaining = this.RESPONSE_TIMEOUT_MS;
 835:         const timerEl = document.getElementById('response-timer');
 836: 
 837:         this.responseTimeout = setInterval(() => {
 838:             remaining -= 1000;
 839:             if (timerEl) {
 840:                 timerEl.textContent = `${Math.ceil(remaining / 1000)}s`;
 841:                 if (remaining <= 5000) {
 842:                     timerEl.style.color = '#e74c3c';
 843:                 }
 844:             }
 845: 
 846:             if (remaining <= 0) {
 847:                 this.clearResponseTimeout();
 848:                 // Time's up - force resolve
 849:                 const myIndex = typeof myPlayerIndex !== 'undefined' ? myPlayerIndex : activePlayerIndex;
 850:                 const isCasterClient = !(typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer) || myIndex === this.currentCaster;
 851:                 if (isCasterClient) {
 852:                     // Caster: all remaining players are treated as passes — force resolve
 853:                     console.log('⏰ Response timeout on caster client — force resolving');
 854:                     this.resolveResponseStack();
 855:                 } else {
 856:                     // Non-caster: auto-pass
 857:                     this.playerPasses(myIndex);
 858:                 }
 859:             }
 860:         }, 1000);
 861:     }
 862: 
 863:     /**
 864:      * Clear the response timeout
 865:      */
 866:     clearResponseTimeout() {
 867:         if (this.responseTimeout) {
 868:             clearInterval(this.responseTimeout);
 869:             this.responseTimeout = null;
 870:         }
 871:     }
 872: 
 873:     /**
 874:      * Show error message in response modal
 875:      */
 876:     showResponseError(message) {
 877:         const modal = document.getElementById('response-window-modal');
 878:         if (!modal) return;
 879: 
 880:         const errorDiv = document.createElement('div');
 881:         Object.assign(errorDiv.style, {
 882:             backgroundColor: '#e74c3c',
 883:             color: 'white',
 884:             padding: '10px',
 885:             borderRadius: '5px',
 886:             textAlign: 'center',
 887:             marginBottom: '10px'
 888:         });
 889:         errorDiv.textContent = message;
 890:         modal.insertBefore(errorDiv, modal.firstChild);
 891: 
 892:         setTimeout(() => errorDiv.remove(), 3000);
 893:     }
 894: 
 895:     // Helper methods that interface with the game state
 896:     getPlayerAP(playerIndex) {
 897:         // In multiplayer, each player tracks their own AP via playerAPs
 898:         if (typeof playerAPs !== 'undefined' && playerAPs[playerIndex]) {
 899:             const ap = playerAPs[playerIndex];
 900:             return (ap.currentAP || 0) + (ap.voidAP || 0);
 901:         }
 902:         // Fallback to global currentAP for single player or active player
 903:         if (typeof currentAP !== 'undefined') {
 904:             const voidAP = typeof voidAPCount !== 'undefined' ? voidAPCount : 0;
 905:             return currentAP + voidAP;
 906:         }
 907:         return 0;
 908:     }
 909: 
 910:     spendPlayerAP(playerIndex, amount) {
 911:         if (typeof spendAP === 'function') {
 912:             spendAP(amount);
 913:         }
 914:     }
 915: 
 916:     getPlayerPosition(playerIndex) {
 917:         if (typeof playerPositions !== 'undefined' && playerPositions[playerIndex]) {
 918:             return playerPositions[playerIndex];
 919:         }
 920:         return null;
 921:     }
 922: 
 923:     getPlayerName(playerIndex) {
 924:         if (typeof playerPositions !== 'undefined' && playerPositions[playerIndex]?.username) {
 925:             return playerPositions[playerIndex].username;
 926:         }
 927:         return `Player ${playerIndex + 1}`;
 928:     }
 929: 
 930:     getElementColor(element) {
 931:         const colors = {
 932:             earth: '#69d83a',
 933:             water: '#5894f4',
 934:             fire: '#ed1b43',
 935:             wind: '#ffce00',
 936:             void: '#9458f4',
 937:             catacomb: '#9b59b6'
 938:         };
 939:         return colors[element] || '#ffffff';
 940:     }
 941: 
 942:     checkPatternForPlayer(scrollName, playerIndex) {
 943:         // Delegate to spell system's pattern check for specific player
 944:         if (this.spellSystem && typeof this.spellSystem.checkPatternForPlayer === 'function') {
 945:             return this.spellSystem.checkPatternForPlayer(scrollName, playerIndex);
 946:         }
 947:         // Fallback to basic check if method doesn't exist
 948:         if (this.spellSystem && typeof this.spellSystem.checkPattern === 'function') {
 949:             return this.spellSystem.checkPattern(scrollName);
 950:         }
 951:         return false;
 952:     }
 953: 
 954:     // Multiplayer broadcast methods
 955:     broadcastResponseWindowOpened(scrollData, casterIndex) {
 956:         if (typeof broadcastGameAction === 'function') {
 957:             // Include the current common area state so receiving clients can apply it
 958:             // before checking canPlayerRespond — avoids a race condition where
 959:             // common-area-update (e.g. Psychic moving there) hasn't arrived yet.
 960:             const commonAreaSnapshot = this.spellSystem?.commonArea
 961:                 ? { ...this.spellSystem.commonArea }
 962:                 : null;
 963:             broadcastGameAction('response-window-opened', {
 964:                 scrollName: scrollData.name,
 965:                 casterIndex: casterIndex,
 966:                 commonArea: commonAreaSnapshot
 967:             });
 968:         }
 969:     }
 970: 
 971:     broadcastResponse(scrollInfo, playerIndex) {
 972:         if (typeof broadcastGameAction === 'function') {
 973:             broadcastGameAction('scroll-response', {
 974:                 scrollName: scrollInfo.name,
 975:                 playerIndex: playerIndex,
 976:                 isCounter: scrollInfo.isCounter
 977:             });
 978:         }
 979:     }
 980: 
 981:     broadcastPass(playerIndex) {
 982:         if (typeof broadcastGameAction === 'function') {
 983:             broadcastGameAction('response-pass', {
 984:                 playerIndex: playerIndex
 985:             });
 986:         }
 987:     }
 988: 
 989:     broadcastResponseResolved(results, originalScroll = null) {
 990:         if (typeof broadcastGameAction === 'function') {
 991:             // Resolve the original scroll's definition for the broadcast so remote
 992:             // clients can store it (e.g. for Reflect's deferred activation).
 993:             let trigDef = null;
 994:             if (originalScroll) {
 995:                 trigDef = originalScroll.scrollData?.definition
 996:                     || originalScroll.scrollData?.spell
 997:                     || this.spellSystem?.patterns?.[originalScroll.scrollData?.name]
 998:                     || null;
 999:             }
1000:             broadcastGameAction('response-resolved', {
1001:                 results: results.map(r => ({
1002:                     scrollName: r.scrollData?.name,
1003:                     casterIndex: r.casterIndex,
1004:                     result: r.result,
1005:                     isResponse: r.isResponse
1006:                 })),
1007:                 triggeringScroll: originalScroll ? {
1008:                     name: originalScroll.scrollData?.name,
1009:                     casterIndex: originalScroll.casterIndex,
1010:                     definition: trigDef
1011:                 } : null
1012:             });
1013:         }
1014:     }
1015: 
1016:     /**
1017:      * Handle remote response resolution - close window on all clients
1018:      */
1019:     handleRemoteResolved() {
1020:         console.log('Remote response window resolved');
1021:         this.clearResponseTimeout();
1022:         this.closeResponseModal();
1023:         this.isResponseWindowOpen = false;
1024:         this.pendingScrollData = null;
1025:         this.currentCaster = null;
1026:         this.respondingPlayers.clear();
1027:     }
1028: 
1029:     // Methods for handling remote multiplayer events
1030: 
1031:     /**
1032:      * Show response modal for a non-casting player (called when receiving broadcast)
1033:      */
1034:     showResponseModalForOtherPlayer(scrollData, casterIndex) {
1035:         this.isResponseWindowOpen = true;
1036:         this.currentCaster = casterIndex;
1037:         this.pendingScrollData = scrollData;
1038:         this.respondingPlayers.clear();
1039:         // The caster is already considered to have "responded" (they cast the spell)
1040:         this.respondingPlayers.add(casterIndex);
1041:         this.responseStack = [{
1042:             scrollData,
1043:             casterIndex,
1044:             isOriginal: true
1045:         }];
1046: 
1047:         // Only show the modal if THIS player can respond; otherwise auto-pass
1048:         const myIndex = typeof myPlayerIndex !== 'undefined' ? myPlayerIndex : activePlayerIndex;
1049:         const responseCheck = this.canPlayerRespond(myIndex);
1050:         if (responseCheck.canRespond) {
1051:             this.showResponseModal();
1052:             this.startResponseTimeout();
1053:         } else {
1054:             if (typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer) {
1055:                 this.broadcastPass(myIndex);
1056:             }
1057:             // Mark as responded locally so caster can resolve
1058:             this.respondingPlayers.add(myIndex);
1059:             this.checkAllPlayersResponded();
1060:         }
1061:     }
1062: 
1063:     /**
1064:      * Handle a remote player passing
1065:      */
1066:     handleRemotePass(playerIndex) {
1067:         console.log(`Remote player ${playerIndex} passed`);
1068:         this.respondingPlayers.add(playerIndex);
1069: 
1070:         // Check if all non-caster players have responded
1071:         this.checkAllPlayersResponded();
1072:     }
1073: 
1074:     /**
1075:      * Handle a remote player responding with a scroll
1076:      * NOTE: Only ONE response is allowed - resolve immediately after receiving it
1077:      */
1078:     handleRemoteResponse(scrollName, playerIndex, isCounter) {
1079:         console.log(`Remote player ${playerIndex} responded with ${scrollName}`);
1080: 
1081:         const scrollDef = this.spellSystem.patterns[scrollName];
1082:         this.responseStack.push({
1083:             scrollData: { name: scrollName, definition: scrollDef },
1084:             casterIndex: playerIndex,
1085:             isCounter: isCounter,
1086:             isResponse: scrollDef?.isResponse || false,
1087:             isOriginal: false
1088:         });
1089: 
1090:         // Close current modal
1091:         this.closeResponseModal();
1092: 
1093:         // Only the caster's client should resolve the response stack.
1094:         // Non-caster clients wait for the 'response-resolved' broadcast.
1095:         const myIndex = typeof myPlayerIndex !== 'undefined' ? myPlayerIndex : null;
1096:         const isCasterClient = !(typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer) || myIndex === this.currentCaster;
1097:         if (isCasterClient) {
1098:             // Only ONE response allowed - immediately resolve the stack
1099:             console.log(`  One response received from remote player (caster client), resolving stack immediately`);
1100:             this.resolveResponseStack();
1101:         } else {
1102:             console.log(`  Response received, waiting for caster to resolve`);
1103:             this.clearResponseTimeout();
1104:         }
1105:     }
1106: 
1107:     /**
1108:      * Check if all players have responded and resolve if so
1109:      */
1110:     checkAllPlayersResponded() {
1111:         // Only the caster's client should resolve the response stack.
1112:         // Non-caster clients wait for the 'response-resolved' broadcast.
1113:         const myIndex = typeof myPlayerIndex !== 'undefined' ? myPlayerIndex : null;
1114:         const isCasterClient = !(typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer) || myIndex === this.currentCaster;
1115:         if (!isCasterClient) {
1116:             return; // Non-caster: do nothing, wait for broadcast
1117:         }
1118: 
1119:         const numPlayers = typeof playerPositions !== 'undefined' ? playerPositions.length : 1;
1120: 
1121:         // Count how many non-caster players should respond
1122:         let expectedResponders = 0;
1123:         for (let i = 0; i < numPlayers; i++) {
1124:             if (i !== this.currentCaster) {
1125:                 expectedResponders++;
1126:             }
1127:         }
1128: 
1129:         const requiredResponders = expectedResponders + 1; // include caster (already counted)
1130:         console.log(`Response check: ${this.respondingPlayers.size} responded, ${requiredResponders} expected`);
1131: 
1132:         if (this.respondingPlayers.size >= requiredResponders) {
1133:             // All players have responded - resolve the stack
1134:             this.resolveResponseStack();
1135:         }
1136:     }
1137: }
1138: 
1139: // Export for use
1140: if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
1141:     window.ResponseWindowSystem = ResponseWindowSystem;
1142: }
</file>

<file path="js/scrolls/scroll-definitions.js">
  1: /**
  2:  * Scroll Definitions for Godaigo
  3:  *
  4:  * This file contains all scroll pattern definitions.
  5:  * Each scroll has:
  6:  * - name: Display name
  7:  * - description: What the scroll does
  8:  * - level: Power level (affects stone gains)
  9:  * - element: The element type (earth, water, fire, wind, void, catacomb)
 10:  * - patterns: Array of valid pattern configurations (relative hex coordinates)
 11:  * - canCounter: (optional) Special scrolls that can counter other scrolls
 12:  */
 13: 
 14: // Pattern templates shared across element types
 15: const LEVEL_1_PATTERNS = [
 16:     [{ q: 0, r: -1 }, { q: 0, r: 1 }],
 17:     [{ q: 1, r: -1 }, { q: -1, r: 1 }],
 18:     [{ q: 1, r: 0 }, { q: -1, r: 0 }]
 19: ];
 20: 
 21: const LEVEL_2_PATTERNS = [
 22:     [{ q: -1, r: -1 }, { q: 1, r: 1 }],
 23:     [{ q: 1, r: -2 }, { q: -1, r: 2 }],
 24:     [{ q: 2, r: -1 }, { q: -2, r: 1 }]
 25: ];
 26: 
 27: const LEVEL_3_PATTERNS = [
 28:     [{ q: 1, r: -1 }, { q: -1, r: 0 }, { q: 0, r: 1 }],
 29:     [{ q: 0, r: -1 }, { q: 1, r: 0 }, { q: -1, r: 1 }]
 30: ];
 31: 
 32: const LEVEL_4_PATTERNS = [
 33:     [{ q: 1, r: -2 }, { q: -2, r: 1 }, { q: 1, r: 1 }],
 34:     [{ q: -1, r: -1 }, { q: 2, r: -1 }, { q: -1, r: 2 }]
 35: ];
 36: 
 37: const LEVEL_5_PATTERNS = [
 38:     [{ q: 0, r: -1 }, { q: 0, r: 1 }, { q: 2, r: -1 }, { q: -2, r: 1 }],
 39:     [{ q: 1, r: -1 }, { q: -1, r: 1 }, { q: -1, r: -1 }, { q: 1, r: 1 }],
 40:     [{ q: 1, r: 0 }, { q: -1, r: 0 }, { q: 1, r: -2 }, { q: -1, r: 2 }]
 41: ];
 42: 
 43: // Helper to convert Roman numerals
 44: function toRoman(num) {
 45:     const romans = ['I', 'II', 'III', 'IV', 'V'];
 46:     return romans[num - 1] || num.toString();
 47: }
 48: 
 49: // Helper function to rotate hex coordinate by 60 degrees
 50: function rotateHex(q, r, steps) {
 51:     let nq = q, nr = r;
 52:     for (let i = 0; i < steps; i++) {
 53:         const tempQ = nq;
 54:         nq = -nr;
 55:         nr = -(-tempQ - nr);
 56:     }
 57:     return { q: nq, r: nr };
 58: }
 59: 
 60: // Earth scroll effect definitions
 61: const EARTH_SCROLL_EFFECTS = {
 62:     1: { name: 'Iron Stance', description: 'Counter the most recently cast scroll. That scroll is cancelled.', isCounter: true },
 63:     2: { name: 'Shifting Sands', description: 'Select two tiles to swap their positions. Tiles must have no stones or players on them.' },
 64:     3: { name: "Mason's Savvy", description: 'Draw up to 5 earth stones. This turn, place earth stones within 5 hexes of player.' },
 65:     4: { name: 'Heavy Stomp', description: 'Select a tile to flip. Hidden tiles are revealed (draw scroll). Revealed tiles become hidden.' },
 66:     5: { name: 'Avalanche', description: 'This turn, place any stones anywhere on the board (not just adjacent).' }
 67: };

 71:     1: { name: 'Reflect', description: 'Duplicate the effect of the scroll that was last cast this turn.', isResponse: true },
 72:     2: { name: 'Refreshing Thought', description: 'Draw a Catacomb scroll.' },
 73:     3: { name: 'Inspiring Draught', description: 'Draw 2 scrolls from any decks, then put 1 back and shuffle that deck.' },
 74:     4: { name: 'Wandering River', description: 'Select a tile. Until your next turn, that tile counts as any element type you choose.' },
 75:     5: { name: 'Control the Current', description: 'Click adjacent water stones to transform them into any other element (free, no AP cost).' }
 76: };

 81:     2: { name: 'Burning Motivation', description: 'Until end of turn, gain 2 AP for each stone you place. Stacks if activated multiple times.' },
 82:     3: { name: 'Sacrificial Pyre', description: 'Activate any scroll in your hand (ignoring pattern). The scroll goes to the common area.' },
 83:     4: { name: 'Transmute', description: 'Discard any number of stones or scrolls to regain 2 AP each.' },
 84:     5: { name: 'Arson', description: 'Destroy one elemental stone from an opponent\'s pool. Move Arson to the common area.' }

 89:     1: { name: 'Psychic', description: 'Counter the previous scroll, then play it during your turn. Move Psychic to the common area.', isCounter: true },
 90:     2: { name: "Scholar's Insight", description: 'Search through a Scroll Deck and add a scroll of your choice to your hand. Shuffle that deck afterwards.' },
 91:     3: { name: 'Simplify', description: 'Scrolls cost 1 AP for you to cast until the beginning of your next turn.' },
 92:     4: { name: 'Telekinesis', description: 'Move a tile unoccupied by stones or players. It must be touching 1 other tile. Cannot move a tile if it would strand an adjacent tile.' },

 94: };

 98:     1: { name: 'Sigh of Recollection', description: 'Draw a scroll and a stone of the type that was just activated, if available. Draw one stone of each if it was a Catacomb scroll.', isResponse: true },
 99:     2: { name: 'Respirate', description: 'Draw 2 wind stones. At end of turn, return all your wind stones to the source pools.' },
100:     3: { name: 'Breath of Power', description: 'Until end of turn, you may move adjacent stones to another adjacent empty space.' },
101:     4: { name: 'Take Flight', description: 'Teleport target player to an unoccupied space of your choice. If targeting an opponent, Take Flight goes to their hand. If targeting yourself, it stays in your active area.' },
102:     5: { name: 'Freedom', description: 'Until your next turn, the centers of elemental shrines act as catacomb tiles (only applies to you).' }
103: };

106: const CATACOMB_SCROLL_EFFECTS = {
107:     1: { name: 'Mudslide', description: 'Until end of turn, earth and water stones act as wind stones for movement (free movement).' },
108:     2: { name: 'Mine', description: 'If the center of this pattern is an elemental shrine, that shrine produces twice as many stones this turn (cannot exceed 5).' },

115:     9: { name: 'Quick Reflexes', description: 'Search for an available level 1 scroll and add it to your hand. Draw 2 stones of that type. Until your next turn, level 1 scrolls cost 0 AP to activate. Shuffle the deck searched.' },
116:     10: { name: 'Combust', description: 'Select a tile and destroy all stones on it. Cannot target player tiles.' }

119: // Generate elemental scroll definitions
120: function generateElementalScrolls() {
121:     const scrolls = {};
122:     const elementTypes = ['earth', 'water', 'fire', 'wind', 'void'];
123:     const patternLevels = [LEVEL_1_PATTERNS, LEVEL_2_PATTERNS, LEVEL_3_PATTERNS, LEVEL_4_PATTERNS, LEVEL_5_PATTERNS];
124: 
125:     elementTypes.forEach(element => {
126:         patternLevels.forEach((patterns, level) => {
127:             const scrollName = `${element.toUpperCase()}_SCROLL_${level + 1}`;
128: 
129:             // Check for custom effect definitions
130:             let name, description, isCounter = false;
131:             const effectMaps = {
132:                 earth: EARTH_SCROLL_EFFECTS,
133:                 water: WATER_SCROLL_EFFECTS,
134:                 fire: FIRE_SCROLL_EFFECTS,
135:                 wind: WIND_SCROLL_EFFECTS,
136:                 void: VOID_SCROLL_EFFECTS
137:             };
138: 
139:             let isResponse = false;
140:             if (effectMaps[element] && effectMaps[element][level + 1]) {
141:                 const effect = effectMaps[element][level + 1];
142:                 name = effect.name;
143:                 description = effect.description;
144:                 isCounter = effect.isCounter || false;
145:                 isResponse = effect.isResponse || false;
146:             } else {
147:                 name = `${element.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + element.slice(1)} Scroll ${toRoman(level + 1)}`;
148:                 description = `Stand in pattern to gain +${level + 1} ${element} stones (2 AP)`;
149:             }
150: 
151:             scrolls[scrollName] = {
152:                 name: name,
153:                 description: description,
154:                 level: level + 1,
155:                 element: element,
156:                 patterns: patterns.map(pattern =>
157:                     pattern.map(pos => ({ ...pos, type: element }))
158:                 ),
159:                 // Earth I (Iron Stance) can counter any scroll
160:                 canCounter: isCounter ? 'any' : null,
161:                 // Fire I (Unbidden Lamplight) is a response scroll (shows in response window but doesn't cancel)
162:                 isResponse: isResponse,
163:                 hasEffect: !!(effectMaps[element] && effectMaps[element][level + 1]) // Flag to indicate this scroll has a special effect
164:             };
165:         });
166:     });
167: 
168:     return scrolls;
169: }
170: 
171: // Generate catacomb scroll definitions (multi-element patterns)
172: function generateCatacombScrolls() {
173:     const catacombBase = {
174:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_1: [
175:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "water" },
176:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "water" },
177:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "earth" },
178:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "earth" }
179:         ],
180:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_2: [
181:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "earth" },
182:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "earth" },
183:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "fire" },
184:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "fire" }
185:         ],
186:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_3: [
187:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "wind" },
188:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "wind" },
189:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "earth" },
190:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "earth" }
191:         ],
192:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_4: [
193:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "void" },
194:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "void" },
195:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "earth" },
196:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "earth" }
197:         ],
198:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_5: [
199:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "water" },
200:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "water" },
201:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "fire" },
202:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "fire" }
203:         ],
204:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_6: [
205:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "wind" },
206:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "wind" },
207:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "water" },
208:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "water" }
209:         ],
210:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_7: [
211:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "void" },
212:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "void" },
213:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "water" },
214:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "water" }
215:         ],
216:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_8: [
217:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "fire" },
218:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "fire" },
219:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "wind" },
220:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "wind" }
221:         ],
222:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_9: [
223:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "void" },
224:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "void" },
225:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "wind" },
226:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "wind" }
227:         ],
228:         CATACOMB_SCROLL_10: [
229:             { q: -1, r: -1, type: "fire" },
230:             { q: 1, r: 1, type: "fire" },
231:             { q: 1, r: -2, type: "void" },
232:             { q: -1, r: 2, type: "void" }
233:         ]
234:     };
235: 
236:     const scrolls = {};
237: 
238:     Object.entries(catacombBase).forEach(([scrollName, basePattern]) => {
239:         const scrollNum = parseInt(scrollName.split('_')[2]);
240: 
241:         // Create 3 rotational variations (0, 120, 240 degrees)
242:         const variations = [
243:             basePattern,
244:             basePattern.map(pos => ({
245:                 ...rotateHex(pos.q, pos.r, 2),
246:                 type: pos.type
247:             })),
248:             basePattern.map(pos => ({
249:                 ...rotateHex(pos.q, pos.r, 4),
250:                 type: pos.type
251:             }))
252:         ];
253: 
254:         // Check for custom catacomb effect definitions
255:         const catEffect = CATACOMB_SCROLL_EFFECTS[scrollNum];
256:         const catName = catEffect ? catEffect.name : `Catacomb Scroll ${scrollNum}`;
257:         const catDesc = catEffect ? catEffect.description : `Multi-element pattern: gain +2 of each stone type (2 AP)`;
258: 
259:         scrolls[scrollName] = {
260:             name: catName,
261:             description: catDesc,
262:             level: 2,
263:             element: 'catacomb',
264:             patterns: variations,
265:             hasEffect: !!catEffect
266:         };
267:     });
268: 
269:     return scrolls;
270: }
271: 
272: // Build complete scroll definitions
273: const SCROLL_DEFINITIONS = {
274:     ...generateElementalScrolls(),
275:     ...generateCatacombScrolls()
276: };
277: 
278: // Initial deck configurations
279: const SCROLL_DECKS = {
280:     earth: ['EARTH_SCROLL_1', 'EARTH_SCROLL_2', 'EARTH_SCROLL_3', 'EARTH_SCROLL_4', 'EARTH_SCROLL_5'],
281:     water: ['WATER_SCROLL_1', 'WATER_SCROLL_2', 'WATER_SCROLL_3', 'WATER_SCROLL_4', 'WATER_SCROLL_5'],
282:     fire: ['FIRE_SCROLL_1', 'FIRE_SCROLL_2', 'FIRE_SCROLL_3', 'FIRE_SCROLL_4', 'FIRE_SCROLL_5'],
283:     wind: ['WIND_SCROLL_1', 'WIND_SCROLL_2', 'WIND_SCROLL_3', 'WIND_SCROLL_4', 'WIND_SCROLL_5'],
284:     void: ['VOID_SCROLL_1', 'VOID_SCROLL_2', 'VOID_SCROLL_3', 'VOID_SCROLL_4', 'VOID_SCROLL_5'],
285:     catacomb: ['CATACOMB_SCROLL_1', 'CATACOMB_SCROLL_2', 'CATACOMB_SCROLL_3', 'CATACOMB_SCROLL_4', 'CATACOMB_SCROLL_5',
286:                'CATACOMB_SCROLL_6', 'CATACOMB_SCROLL_7', 'CATACOMB_SCROLL_8', 'CATACOMB_SCROLL_9', 'CATACOMB_SCROLL_10']
287: };
288: 
289: // Export for use in other modules
290: if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
291:     window.SCROLL_DEFINITIONS = SCROLL_DEFINITIONS;
292:     window.SCROLL_DECKS = SCROLL_DECKS;
293: }
</file>

<file path="css/INDEX.md">
 1: # css/ — Stylesheet Index
 2: 
 3: > See parent: [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md)
 4: 
 5: ---
 6: 
 7: ## FILE MAP
 8: 
 9: | File | Responsibility |
10: |------|---------------|
11: | `variables.css` | **Design tokens** — all CSS custom properties. Edit here to retheme. |
12: | `base.css` | Global reset, body/html defaults, font-face declarations |
13: | `styles.css` | **Main stylesheet** — game layout, HUD, panels, modals, tutorial system, auth screen, gamification UI, scroll deck, stone cards, CRT overlay, emoji system, responsive rules |
14: | `layout.css` | Lobby wrapper layout, game layout grid (3-col), panel collapse transitions |
15: | `board.css` | SVG board elements: hex tile fills/strokes, stone circles, player pawn markers, shrine markers, snap indicators |
16: | `components.css` | Buttons, inputs, modals, dropdowns, cards, status text, common UI primitives |
17: | `responsive.css` | Media queries for tablet (≤1024px) and mobile (≤768px) |
18: 
19: > **Note:** `styles.css` has grown to absorb most rules. When adding new features, default to `styles.css` unless the rule is clearly board-SVG (`board.css`) or a layout grid change (`layout.css`).
20: 
21: ---
22: 
23: ## variables.css — Token Reference
24: 
25: ```css
26: /* Colors */
27: --bg-dark: #0d0d0d
28: --bg-panel: #181818
29: --bg-card: #222
30: --bg-elevated: #2a2a2a
31: --accent-gold: #e6c79c        ← primary accent; used for borders, highlights
32: --accent-orange: #c05500
33: --text-muted: (implicit ~#666)
34: --border-subtle: (implicit)
35: 
36: /* Spacing */
37: --hud-height, --action-bar-height, --panel-width (used in grid template)
38: 
39: /* Typography */
40: --font-terminal: (VT323 or monospace fallback)
41: --font-pixel: (Press Start 2P)
42: 
43: /* Radius */
44: --radius-lg: 4px
45: ```
46: 
47: ---
48: 
49: ## Z-Index Stack (styles.css)
50: 
51: From lowest to highest. Anything not listed here is `auto` (stacking context order).
52: 
53: | z-index | Element |
54: |---------|---------|
55: | 2000 | `.scroll-browse-content` overlay |
56: | 3000 | `.stone-info-overlay` (stone ability popup) |
57: | 8500 | Gamification toast / notification |
58: | 8600 | `.tutorial-blocking-overlay` (spotlight dim layer) |
59: | 8610 | `.tutorial-modal` (center modals) |
60: | 8620 | `.tutorial-spotlight` (highlighted element) |
61: | 8640 | `.tutorial-modal.tutorial-corner` (corner modals) |
62: | 9000 | `.gami-overlay` (gamification panel) |
63: | 9999 | Various top-level overlays (emoji shop, badge panel) |
64: | 10000 | `.retro-dlg-overlay` (confirm dialogs) |
65: 
66: > Tutorial z-indices were raised from ~2500 to ~8600 to clear the stone info overlay at 3000.
67: 
68: ---
69: 
70: ## Tutorial CSS Classes
71: 
72: All prefixed `tmode-` (new interactive tutorial) or `tutorial-` (shared/legacy):
73: 
74: | Class | Purpose |
75: |-------|---------|
76: | `.tutorial-modal` | Full-screen overlay wrapper (center or corner) |
77: | `.tutorial-modal.tutorial-corner` | Corner-positioned modal variant (no dim bg) |
78: | `.tutorial-modal.tutorial-visible` | opacity:1 (added via rAF after append) |
79: | `.tutorial-modal.tutorial-fade-out` | opacity:0 for exit animation |
80: | `.tutorial-content` | The card box inside the modal |
81: | `.tutorial-blocking-overlay` | Full-screen click-eater for spotlight steps |
82: | `.tutorial-spotlight` | Applied to target element — gold pulse glow, z-index:8620 |
83: | `.tutorial-highlight-ring` | SVG circle on board with pulse animation |
84: | `.tmode-next` | "Continue" / "Got it" button inside tutorial modal |
85: | `.tmode-exit` | ✕ exit button — top-right of every modal |
86: | `.auth-btn-tutorial` | Tutorial entry button on auth screen |
87: | `.auth-tutorial-divider` | "or" divider above tutorial button |
</file>
