<file path="js/bot.js">
  1: // ============================================================
  2: // bot.js — Scripted test bot for Godaigo
  3: // ============================================================
  4: // HOW TO USE:
  5: //   Press Shift+R during an active game to execute one bot action
  6: //   on behalf of the current active player.
  7: //
  8: // PRIORITY ORDER (per Shift+R press):
  9: //   1. Cast a scroll if its pattern is already satisfied
 10: //   2. Place one missing stone toward the best castable pattern
 11: //   3. If standing on a shrine centre → end turn to collect stones
 12: //   4. Move one hex step toward the nearest collectible shrine
 13: //   5. Fallback: end turn
 14: //
 15: // LOAD ORDER: after lobby.js (depends on game-core.js + lobby.js globals)
 16: // ============================================================
 17: 
 18: (function () {
 19:     'use strict';
 20: 
 21:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
 22:     // Logging helper
 23:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
 24:     function log(...args) {
 25:         console.log('🤖 [Bot]', ...args);
 26:     }
 27: 
 28:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
 29:     // Helpers: position of the active player
 30:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
 31:     function botPos() {
 32:         const player = playerPositions[activePlayerIndex];
 33:         if (!player) return null;
 34:         return { x: player.x, y: player.y };
 35:     }
 36: 
 37:     // Convert player pixel position to an integer axial hex coordinate.
 38:     // Uses pixelToHex + hexRound (both defined in game-core.js).
 39:     function botHex(px, py) {
 40:         return pixelToHex(px, py, TILE_SIZE);
 41:     }
 42: 
 43:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
 44:     // BFS: find a walkable path from (sx,sy) to (tx,ty).
 45:     // Returns an array of steps [{x, y, cost}], or null if unreachable.
 46:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
 47:     function botFindPath(sx, sy, tx, ty) {
 48:         const allHexes = getAllHexagonPositions();
 49:         const THRESH = 5;
 50: 
 51:         let startHex = null, endHex = null;
 52:         for (const h of allHexes) {
 53:             if (Math.hypot(h.x - sx, h.y - sy) < THRESH) startHex = h;
 54:             if (Math.hypot(h.x - tx, h.y - ty) < THRESH) endHex   = h;
 55:         }
 56:         if (!startHex || !endHex) return null;
 57:         if (startHex.key === endHex.key) return [];
 58: 
 59:         const visited = new Set([startHex.key]);
 60:         const queue   = [{ hex: startHex, path: [] }];
 61: 
 62:         while (queue.length > 0) {
 63:             const { hex, path } = queue.shift();
 64: 
 65:             // neighbours = all hex positions within one hex step (~35 px)
 66:             const neighbours = allHexes.filter(h => {
 67:                 if (visited.has(h.key)) return false;
 68:                 const d = Math.hypot(h.x - hex.x, h.y - hex.y);
 69:                 return d > THRESH && d < 40;
 70:             });
 71: 
 72:             for (const nb of neighbours) {
 73:                 const mv = canPlayerMoveToHex(nb.x, nb.y, false);
 74:                 if (!mv.canMove) continue;
 75: 
 76:                 const step = { x: nb.x, y: nb.y, cost: mv.cost ?? 1 };
 77:                 const newPath = [...path, step];
 78: 
 79:                 if (nb.key === endHex.key) return newPath;
 80: 
 81:                 visited.add(nb.key);
 82:                 queue.push({ hex: nb, path: newPath });
 83:             }
 84:         }
 85:         return null; // unreachable
 86:     }
 87: 
 88:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
 89:     // Move the active player one hex step (direct state update).
 90:     // Mirrors what movePlayerAlongPath() does without requiring a drag.
 91:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
 92:     function botMoveStep(x, y, cost) {
 93:         const pi     = activePlayerIndex;
 94:         const player = playerPositions[pi];
 95:         if (!player) { log('Player pawn not found'); return false; }
 96: 
 97:         if (getTotalAP() < cost) {
 98:             log(`Not enough AP (need ${cost}, have ${getTotalAP()})`);
 99:             return false;
100:         }
101: 
102:         // Update pawn position
103:         player.x = x;
104:         player.y = y;
105:         player.element.setAttribute('transform', `translate(${x}, ${y})`);
106: 
107:         // Spend AP + update HUD
108:         spendAP(cost);
109: 
110:         // Reveal tiles underneath (lobby.js)
111:         if (typeof handlePlayerLanding === 'function') handlePlayerLanding(x, y);
112: 
113:         // Broadcast in multiplayer (lobby.js)
114:         if (typeof broadcastPlayerMovement === 'function') {
115:             broadcastPlayerMovement(pi, x, y, cost);
116:         }
117: 
118:         log(`Moved to (${x.toFixed(1)}, ${y.toFixed(1)}) — cost ${cost} AP, ${getTotalAP()} AP remaining`);
119:         return true;
120:     }
121: 
122:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
123:     // Find the nearest revealed elemental shrine whose pool isn't full.
124:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
125:     const ELEMENTAL_TYPES = ['earth', 'water', 'fire', 'wind', 'void'];
126: 
127:     function botFindTargetShrine() {
128:         const pos = botPos();
129:         if (!pos) return null;
130: 
131:         let best = null, bestDist = Infinity;
132: 
133:         for (const tile of placedTiles) {
134:             if (tile.isPlayerTile)                                    continue;
135:             if (tile.flipped)                                         continue; // unvisited
136:             if (!ELEMENTAL_TYPES.includes(tile.shrineType))           continue;
137: 
138:             // Skip if player pool is already at capacity for this type
139:             const cap = (typeof playerPoolCapacity !== 'undefined' ? playerPoolCapacity : {})[tile.shrineType] ?? 5;
140:             if ((playerPool[tile.shrineType] || 0) >= cap)            continue;
141: 
142:             const d = Math.hypot(tile.x - pos.x, tile.y - pos.y);
143:             if (d < bestDist) { bestDist = d; best = tile; }
144:         }
145:         return best;
146:     }
147: 
148:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
149:     // Find the best scroll to build toward.
150:     //
151:     // Returns { scrollName, stones: [{x, y, type}], def } where
152:     // `stones` lists the pixel positions that need stones placed for
153:     // the chosen pattern variant, OR null if nothing buildable.
154:     //
155:     // Uses the same hex-round math as checkPattern() so positions
156:     // are guaranteed to match when castSpell() validates them.
157:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
158:     function botPickScrollTarget() {
159:         if (!window.spellSystem || !window.SCROLL_DEFINITIONS) return null;
160: 
161:         const pos = botPos();
162:         if (!pos) return null;
163: 
164:         const playerHex = botHex(pos.x, pos.y);
165:         const { hand }  = window.spellSystem.getPlayerScrolls(false);
166:         const allHexes  = getAllHexagonPositions();
167: 
168:         for (const scrollName of hand) {
169:             const def = window.SCROLL_DEFINITIONS[scrollName];
170:             if (!def || !Array.isArray(def.patterns)) continue;
171: 
172:             // Level 1 scrolls can only be used as responses — skip
173:             if (def.level === 1) continue;
174: 
175:             for (const variant of def.patterns) {
176:                 // Compute exact pixel positions using the same formula as checkPattern()
177:                 const stonePositions = variant.map(req => {
178:                     const px = hexToPixel(playerHex.q + req.q, playerHex.r + req.r, TILE_SIZE);
179:                     return { x: px.x, y: px.y, type: req.type };
180:                 });
181: 
182:                 // All positions must land on valid hex cells on the board
183:                 const allOnBoard = stonePositions.every(sp =>
184:                     allHexes.some(h => Math.hypot(h.x - sp.x, h.y - sp.y) < 5)
185:                 );
186:                 if (!allOnBoard) continue;
187: 
188:                 // Bot must have at least one stone that still needs placing
189:                 const anyMissing = stonePositions.some(sp => {
190:                     const alreadyThere = placedStones.some(
191:                         s => s.type === sp.type && Math.hypot(s.x - sp.x, s.y - sp.y) < 5
192:                     );
193:                     return !alreadyThere && (playerPool[sp.type] || 0) > 0;
194:                 });
195: 
196:                 if (anyMissing) {
197:                     return { scrollName, stones: stonePositions, def };
198:                 }
199:             }
200:         }
201:         return null;
202:     }
203: 
204:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
205:     // Check if the current stone layout satisfies ANY castable scroll
206:     // that the bot has (hand or active area). Returns the scroll name
207:     // or null.
208:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
209:     function botFindCastableScroll() {
210:         if (!window.spellSystem) return null;
211: 
212:         const { hand, active } = window.spellSystem.getPlayerScrolls(false);
213:         const candidates       = [...active, ...hand]; // prefer already-active scrolls
214: 
215:         for (const name of candidates) {
216:             if (window.spellSystem.checkPattern(name)) return name;
217:         }
218:         return null;
219:     }
220: 
221:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
222:     // Place one stone for the bot's target pattern.
223:     // Deducts from playerPool + updates UI + syncs MP state.
224:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
225:     function botPlaceStone(x, y, type) {
226:         if ((playerPool[type] || 0) <= 0) {
227:             log(`No ${type} stones in pool`);
228:             return false;
229:         }
230: 
231:         log(`Placing ${type} stone at (${x.toFixed(1)}, ${y.toFixed(1)})`);
232:         placeStone(x, y, type);
233: 
234:         // Deduct from player pool (mirrors game-ui.js drag-drop logic)
235:         playerPool[type]--;
236:         if (typeof updateStoneCount === 'function') updateStoneCount(type);
237:         if (typeof syncPlayerState  === 'function') syncPlayerState();
238: 
239:         return true;
240:     }
241: 
242:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
243:     // Main bot action — called once per Shift+R press.
244:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
245:     function botAct() {
246:         const pos = botPos();
247:         if (!pos) {
248:             log('Active player pawn not found on board');
249:             return;
250:         }
251: 
252:         const px = pos.x, py = pos.y;
253:         log(`=== Bot step | pos=(${px.toFixed(1)},${py.toFixed(1)}) AP=${getTotalAP()} ===`);
254: 
255:         // ── Priority 1: Cast if pattern is satisfied ─────────────────
256:         const castable = botFindCastableScroll();
257:         if (castable) {
258:             // Ensure the scroll is in the active area before casting
259:             const { hand } = window.spellSystem.getPlayerScrolls(false);
260:             if (hand.has(castable)) {
261:                 window.spellSystem.moveToActive(castable);
262:             }
263:             log(`Casting ${castable}`);
264:             window.spellSystem.castSpell();
265:             return;
266:         }
267: 
268:         // ── Priority 2: Place one stone toward a scroll pattern ───────
269:         const scrollTarget = botPickScrollTarget();
270:         if (scrollTarget) {
271:             for (const sp of scrollTarget.stones) {
272:                 const alreadyThere = placedStones.some(
273:                     s => s.type === sp.type && Math.hypot(s.x - sp.x, s.y - sp.y) < 5
274:                 );
275:                 if (!alreadyThere && (playerPool[sp.type] || 0) > 0) {
276:                     botPlaceStone(sp.x, sp.y, sp.type);
277:                     return;
278:                 }
279:             }
280:         }
281: 
282:         // ── Priority 3: Collect — if on shrine centre, end turn ───────
283:         const onShrine = placedTiles.find(t =>
284:             !t.isPlayerTile &&
285:             !t.flipped &&
286:             ELEMENTAL_TYPES.includes(t.shrineType) &&
287:             Math.hypot(t.x - px, t.y - py) < 5
288:         );
289:         if (onShrine) {
290:             log(`On ${onShrine.shrineType} shrine centre — ending turn to collect`);
291:             const btn = document.getElementById('end-turn');
292:             if (btn && !btn.disabled) btn.click();
293:             return;
294:         }
295: 
296:         // ── Priority 4: Move one step toward nearest shrine ───────────
297:         if (getTotalAP() > 0) {
298:             const shrine = botFindTargetShrine();
299:             if (shrine) {
300:                 const path = botFindPath(px, py, shrine.x, shrine.y);
301:                 if (path && path.length > 0) {
302:                     const step = path[0];
303:                     if (getTotalAP() >= step.cost) {
304:                         botMoveStep(step.x, step.y, step.cost);
305:                         return;
306:                     }
307:                 } else if (path && path.length === 0) {
308:                     // Already at shrine (shouldn't reach here due to priority 3, but be safe)
309:                     log('Already at shrine (path empty), ending turn');
310:                     const btn = document.getElementById('end-turn');
311:                     if (btn && !btn.disabled) btn.click();
312:                     return;
313:                 } else {
314:                     log(`No walkable path to ${shrine.shrineType} shrine`);
315:                 }
316:             } else {
317:                 log('No collectible shrine found (all full or no revealed shrines)');
318:             }
319:         } else {
320:             log('Out of AP — cannot move');
321:         }
322: 
323:         // ── Priority 5: Fallback — end turn ───────────────────────────
324:         log('No better action available — ending turn');
325:         const btn = document.getElementById('end-turn');
326:         if (btn && !btn.disabled) {
327:             btn.click();
328:         } else {
329:             log('End turn button not available (may already be disabled or not your turn)');
330:         }
331:     }
332: 
333:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
334:     // Keyboard binding: Shift+R
335:     // Same guards as game-ui.js to prevent firing in lobby / text inputs.
336:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
337:     document.addEventListener('keydown', function (e) {
338:         if (e.key !== 'R' || !e.shiftKey) return;
339: 
340:         // Don't fire while typing
341:         const tag = document.activeElement?.tagName;
342:         if (tag === 'INPUT' || tag === 'TEXTAREA' || document.activeElement?.isContentEditable) return;
343: 
344:         // Don't fire unless the game is actually running
345:         if (!document.getElementById('game-layout')?.classList.contains('active')) return;
346: 
347:         e.preventDefault();
348:         log('Shift+R pressed — executing one bot step');
349:         botAct();
350:     });
351: 
352:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
353:     // Public API (for browser-console debugging)
354:     // ----------------------------------------------------------------
355:     window.BotSystem = {
356:         /** Manually trigger one bot step */
357:         step:        botAct,
358:         /** Return the nearest collectible shrine tile */
359:         findShrine:  botFindTargetShrine,
360:         /** Return current bot (active player) position */
361:         pos:         botPos,
362:         /** Return the scroll the bot is currently building toward */
363:         pickScroll:  botPickScrollTarget,
364:         /** Check if any scroll is castable right now */
365:         castable:    botFindCastableScroll,
366:     };
367: 
368:     log('Loaded — press Shift+R in-game to advance one bot step');
369: 
370: })();
</file>

<file path="js/crt-overlay.js">
  1: // ============================================================
  2: // CRT OVERLAY  (js/crt-overlay.js)
  3: //
  4: // Canvas-based CRT effects drawn over the entire page.
  5: // Effects: scanlines, vignette, film grain, flicker.
  6: // All effects are on by default and individually togglable.
  7: //
  8: // Storage key: godaigo_crt_${userId}  (or godaigo_crt_guest)
  9: // Public API:  window.crtOverlay.init()
 10: //              window.crtOverlay.setOption(key, bool)
 11: //              window.crtOverlay.getOptions()
 12: //              window.crtOverlay.loadForUser(userId)
 13: //              window.crtOverlay.saveForUser(userId)
 14: // ============================================================
 15: 
 16: window.crtOverlay = (function () {
 17: 
 18:     // ── Internal state ────────────────────────────────────────
 19:     let _canvas = null;
 20:     let _ctx    = null;
 21:     let _raf    = null;
 22: 
 23:     const DEFAULTS = { scanlines: true, vignette: true, grain: true, flicker: true };
 24:     let _opts = { ...DEFAULTS };
 25: 
 26:     // Vignette gradient — rebuilt on resize, reused every frame
 27:     let _vignetteGrad = null;
 28: 
 29:     // Scanline pattern — rebuilt on resize, reused every frame
 30:     let _scanPattern = null;
 31: 
 32:     // Offscreen grain canvas (256×256, tiled)
 33:     let _grainCanvas  = null;
 34:     let _grainCtx     = null;
 35:     let _grainPattern = null;
 36:     let _grainFrame   = 0;
 37: 
 38:     // Flicker state
 39:     let _flickerAlpha = 0;
 40: 
 41:     // ── Storage ───────────────────────────────────────────────
 42: 
 43:     function _key(userId) {
 44:         return userId ? `godaigo_crt_${userId}` : 'godaigo_crt_guest';
 45:     }
 46: 
 47:     function loadForUser(userId) {
 48:         try {
 49:             const raw = localStorage.getItem(_key(userId));
 50:             if (raw) {
 51:                 const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
 52:                 Object.keys(DEFAULTS).forEach(k => {
 53:                     if (typeof parsed[k] === 'boolean') _opts[k] = parsed[k];
 54:                 });
 55:             } else {
 56:                 // No saved prefs — use defaults
 57:                 _opts = { ...DEFAULTS };
 58:             }
 59:         } catch (e) {
 60:             console.warn('[crt-overlay] failed to load settings:', e);
 61:             _opts = { ...DEFAULTS };
 62:         }
 63:     }
 64: 
 65:     function saveForUser(userId) {
 66:         try {
 67:             localStorage.setItem(_key(userId), JSON.stringify(_opts));
 68:         } catch (e) {
 69:             console.warn('[crt-overlay] failed to save settings:', e);
 70:         }
 71:     }
 72: 
 73:     // ── Canvas setup ──────────────────────────────────────────
 74: 
 75:     function init() {
 76:         if (_raf) { cancelAnimationFrame(_raf); _raf = null; }
 77:         if (document.getElementById('crt-canvas')) {
 78:             // Already exists — just restart the loop
 79:             _canvas = document.getElementById('crt-canvas');
 80:             _ctx    = _canvas.getContext('2d');
 81:             _onResize();
 82:             _loop();
 83:             return;
 84:         }
 85: 
 86:         _canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
 87:         _canvas.id = 'crt-canvas';
 88:         _canvas.style.cssText = [
 89:             'position:fixed',
 90:             'inset:0',
 91:             'width:100%',
 92:             'height:100%',
 93:             'z-index:9999',
 94:             'pointer-events:none',
 95:             'display:block'
 96:         ].join(';');
 97:         document.body.appendChild(_canvas);
 98:         _ctx = _canvas.getContext('2d');
 99: 
100:         // Grain offscreen canvas
101:         _grainCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
102:         _grainCanvas.width  = 256;
103:         _grainCanvas.height = 256;
104:         _grainCtx = _grainCanvas.getContext('2d');
105: 
106:         window.addEventListener('resize', _onResize);
107:         // Defer resize to next frame so the canvas is fully laid out
108:         requestAnimationFrame(() => { _onResize(); _loop(); });
109:     }
110: 
111:     function _onResize() {
112:         const w = window.innerWidth;
113:         const h = window.innerHeight;
114:         _canvas.width  = w;
115:         _canvas.height = h;
116:         _vignetteGrad  = _buildVignetteGrad(w, h);
117:         _scanPattern   = _buildScanPattern();
118:         // Grain pattern rebuilt next grain frame
119:         _grainPattern  = null;
120:     }
121: 
122:     // ── Effect builders ───────────────────────────────────────
123: 
124:     function _buildVignetteGrad(w, h) {
125:         const grad = _ctx.createRadialGradient(
126:             w / 2, h / 2, 0,
127:             w / 2, h / 2, Math.max(w, h) * 0.72
128:         );
129:         grad.addColorStop(0,    'rgba(0,0,0,0)');
130:         grad.addColorStop(0.55, 'rgba(0,0,0,0)');
131:         grad.addColorStop(1.0,  'rgba(0,0,0,0.55)');
132:         return grad;
133:     }
134: 
135:     function _buildScanPattern() {
136:         // Realistic CRT phosphor scanlines: 4px period with soft gradient falloff.
137:         // Real CRTs have a dark inter-line gap that feathers into the bright phosphor
138:         // area rather than a hard edge. Four opacity steps simulate this:
139:         //   y=0  gap (darkest)   → y=1  soft shadow   → y=2  barely visible
140:         //   → y=3  clear (phosphor active — full brightness)
141:         // Max opacity (0.09) is half the old flat-line value (0.18).
142:         const sc = document.createElement('canvas');
143:         sc.width  = 1;
144:         sc.height = 4;
145:         const c = sc.getContext('2d');
146:         c.clearRect(0, 0, 1, 4);
147:         c.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.09)';  // inter-line gap — darkest
148:         c.fillRect(0, 0, 1, 1);
149:         c.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.04)';  // soft shadow
150:         c.fillRect(0, 1, 1, 1);
151:         c.fillStyle = 'rgba(0,0,0,0.01)';  // barely visible transition
152:         c.fillRect(0, 2, 1, 1);
153:         // y=3: left transparent — phosphor center at full brightness
154:         return _ctx.createPattern(sc, 'repeat');
155:     }
156: 
157:     function _updateGrain() {
158:         const iw = _grainCanvas.width;
159:         const ih = _grainCanvas.height;
160:         const imageData = _grainCtx.createImageData(iw, ih);
161:         const data = imageData.data;
162:         for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i += 4) {
163:             const v = (Math.random() * 40) | 0;
164:             data[i]     = v;
165:             data[i + 1] = v;
166:             data[i + 2] = v;
167:             data[i + 3] = Math.random() < 0.45 ? 22 : 0;
168:         }
169:         _grainCtx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);
170:         _grainPattern = _ctx.createPattern(_grainCanvas, 'repeat');
171:     }
172: 
173:     // ── Render loop ───────────────────────────────────────────
174: 
175:     function _loop() {
176:         const ctx = _ctx;
177:         const w   = _canvas.width;
178:         const h   = _canvas.height;
179: 
180:         ctx.clearRect(0, 0, w, h);
181: 
182:         if (_opts.flicker)   _drawFlicker(ctx, w, h);
183:         if (_opts.vignette)  _drawVignette(ctx, w, h);
184:         if (_opts.grain)     _drawGrain(ctx, w, h);
185:         if (_opts.scanlines) _drawScanlines(ctx, w, h);
186: 
187:         _raf = requestAnimationFrame(_loop);
188:     }
189: 
190:     function _drawFlicker(ctx, w, h) {
191:         _flickerAlpha += (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.012;
192:         _flickerAlpha  = Math.max(0, Math.min(0.045, _flickerAlpha));
193:         if (_flickerAlpha > 0.001) {
194:             ctx.fillStyle = `rgba(0,0,0,${_flickerAlpha.toFixed(3)})`;
195:             ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h);
196:         }
197:     }
198: 
199:     function _drawVignette(ctx, w, h) {
200:         if (!_vignetteGrad) _vignetteGrad = _buildVignetteGrad(w, h);
201:         ctx.fillStyle = _vignetteGrad;
202:         ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h);
203:     }
204: 
205:     function _drawGrain(ctx, w, h) {
206:         // Update grain tile every 2nd frame
207:         _grainFrame++;
208:         if (_grainFrame % 2 === 0 || !_grainPattern) {
209:             _updateGrain();
210:         }
211:         if (_grainPattern) {
212:             ctx.fillStyle = _grainPattern;
213:             ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h);
214:         }
215:     }
216: 
217:     function _drawScanlines(ctx, w, h) {
218:         if (!_scanPattern) _scanPattern = _buildScanPattern();
219:         ctx.fillStyle = _scanPattern;
220:         ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h);
221:     }
222: 
223:     // ── Public API ────────────────────────────────────────────
224: 
225:     function setOption(key, value) {
226:         if (!(key in DEFAULTS)) return;
227:         _opts[key] = !!value;
228:     }
229: 
230:     function getOptions() {
231:         return { ..._opts };
232:     }
233: 
234:     return { init, setOption, getOptions, loadForUser, saveForUser };
235: 
236: })();
237: 
238: // Auto-init on page load (guest defaults until user signs in)
239: (function () {
240:     function _start() {
241:         window.crtOverlay.loadForUser(null);
242:         window.crtOverlay.init();
243:     }
244:     if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
245:         document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', _start);
246:     } else {
247:         _start();
248:     }
249: })();
</file>

<file path="js/INDEX.md">
  1: # js/ — Module Index
  2: 
  3: > See parent: [CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md)
  4: > Scroll system submodule: [scrolls/INDEX.md](scrolls/INDEX.md)
  5: 
  6: ---
  7: 
  8: ## FILE MAP
  9: 
 10: | File | Lines (approx) | Responsibility |
 11: |------|---------------|---------------|
 12: | `config.js` | ~50 | Supabase client init, game constants (`TILE_SIZE=20`, `STONE_TYPES`, `PLAYER_COLORS`, `stoneCapacity=5`) |
 13: | `game-core.js` | ~5000 | **Core engine**: `SpellSystem` class, tile placement, pawn movement, stone system, AP management, win conditions, turn logic, hex math |
 14: | `game-ui.js` | ~3200 | **UI layer**: drag-drop for pawns/stones/tiles, HUD updates, panel collapse, scroll deck UI, opponent panel, common area |
 15: | `lobby.js` | ~3500 | Auth (Supabase), multiplayer room management, `startGame()`, player ready system, broadcast handlers, mobile UI |
 16: | `multiplayer-state.js` | ~200 | Shared state bucket: `myPlayerId`, `currentGameId`, `isMultiplayer`, `myPlayerIndex`, turn validation helpers |
 17: | `tutorial.js` | ~200 | Legacy 7-step modal (in-game hint, session-dismissable). Do not confuse with `tutorial-mode.js`. |
 18: | `tutorial-mode.js` | ~600 | Interactive guided tutorial: board auto-setup, spotlight system, step machine, movement gating, tile-reveal hooks |
 19: | `parallax.js` | ~100 | Injects animated background layers (space drift + 4 clouds) with CSS keyframes. No game deps. |
 20: | `gamification.js` | ~600 | `window.gami`: XP/gold engine, Supabase profile sync, daily login bonus, `onElementActivated()` |
 21: | `gamification-ui.js` | ~800 | Profile modal (6 tabs: Stats, Colours, Emojis, Badges, Leaderboard, Settings) |
 22: | `emoji-system.js` | ~500 | 83 emojis across 6 gold tiers, purchase validation, pawn overlay display |
 23: | `cosmetics-system.js` | ~300 | Name colour cosmetics (6 tiers), localStorage persistence, equip/purchase logic |
 24: | `crt-overlay.js` | ~400 | Canvas CRT effects: scanlines, vignette, grain, flicker. Per-user Supabase prefs. |
 25: 
 26: ---
 27: 
 28: ## game-core.js — Deep Notes
 29: 
 30: ### SpellSystem class (instantiated as `spellSystem`, exposed as `window.spellSystem`)
 31: - `playerScrolls[]` — per-player inventory: `{hand: Set, active: Set, activated: Set}`
 32: - `scrollDecks{}` — draw piles per element; override via `window.tutorialDeckOverride` before `initializeDeck()`
 33: - `onTileRevealed(element)` — draws top scroll from element deck into active player's hand
 34: - `patternMatchesBoard(scroll, playerHex)` — checks if stone pattern exists around player position
 35: - `activateScroll(scrollId, playerIndex)` — validates pattern + 2 AP, fires effect, tracks win condition
 36: 
 37: ### Key standalone functions
 38: - `hexToPixel(q, r, size)` → `{x, y}` — flat-top hex math. `size=TILE_SIZE(20)` for stones, `size=TILE_SIZE*4(80)` for tiles
 39: - `pixelToHex(x, y, size)` → `{q, r}`
 40: - `placeTile(x, y, rotation, flipped, shrineType, isPlayerTile, skipMP, forcedId)` — creates SVG tile group, assigns color, calls `placePlayer()` for player tiles. Fires `TutorialMode.onPlayerTilePlaced()` hook.
 41: - `revealTile(tileId)` — flips hidden tile: fires `onTilePreReveal` (before visual), builds SVG, fires `onTileRevealed` (after visual), draws scroll
 42: - `addAP(amount)` — respects max AP (5 + void bonus), updates HUD, calls `syncPlayerState()`
 43: - `initializeDeck(numPlayers, seed)` — checks `window.tutorialDeckOverride` first; if set, uses it and nulls the override
 44: - `generateSpiralPositions(n)` — returns world coords for n tiles in spiral order (defined in game-ui.js)
 45: 
 46: ### gotchas
 47: - `placedTiles[]` is the source of truth for board state. `tile.flipped=true` means hidden.
 48: - `activePlayerIndex` governs whose turn it is. In single-player/tutorial, it's always 0.
 49: - `playerPositions[]` entries hold `{x, y, element, color, index}`. Color is always stored as **hex** (`#9b59b6`), not name (`purple`). Bug was COLOR_RANK returning undefined when name stored instead of hex.
 50: - `tileDeck[]` is consumed sequentially (no reshuffle after init). Index tracked by `deckIndex`.
 51: - AP max is 5 by default; each void stone in player pool raises it by 1 (`updateVoidAP()`).
 52: - `skipMultiplayerLogic=true` bypasses broadcast and turn-advance. Used for tutorial cosmetic placements and visual-only syncs from remote broadcasts.
 53: 
 54: ---
 55: 
 56: ## game-ui.js — Deep Notes
 57: 
 58: ### Drag systems (3 independent state machines)
 59: 1. **Pawn drag** (`isDraggingPlayer`) — mousedown on `.player` SVG, constrained by AP and hex grid, calls `revealTile()` on unflipped destination
 60: 2. **Stone drag** (`isDraggingStone`) — mousedown on stone-card or placed stone SVG, drops onto hex grid
 61: 3. **Tile drag** (`isDraggingTile`) — mousedown on `.deck-tile` or placed tile (if `tileMoveMode`), snaps to valid positions
 62: 
 63: ### Tutorial integration in game-ui.js
 64: - Pawn drop handler checks `tutorialBlocked` (allowed hexes set) before accepting move
 65: - Calls `TutorialMode.onPlayerMoved(x, y)` after successful pawn placement
 66: - `startPlayerTileDrag()` is the entry for dragging player tiles from the deck
 67: 
 68: ### generateSpiralPositions(n) — spiral order
 69: Position 0 = `(0,0)` (center). For 1-player tutorial: 6 tiles, positions 0–5. Earth is always at position 0 when `tutorialDeckOverride` is active.
 70: 
 71: ---
 72: 
 73: ## lobby.js — Deep Notes
 74: 
 75: ### startGame(numPlayers) — local single-player launch
 76: 1. `clearBoard()` → reset all state
 77: 2. Hide `#lobby-wrapper`, show `#game-layout`
 78: 3. `initializeDeck(numPlayers)` — checks tutorialDeckOverride
 79: 4. `initializePlayerTiles(numPlayers)` — creates draggable SVG tiles in left panel
 80: 5. Places `numPlayers * 6` tiles via `generateSpiralPositions()` + `placeTile()`
 81: 6. Exposed as `window.startGame` explicitly at bottom of file
 82: 
 83: ### Auth flow
 84: - Page load → `checkAuthSession()` → if session exists, `onAuthSuccess()` immediately (skips auth screen)
 85: - Logged-in users go directly to `#multiplayer-lobby` → `#game-browser-panel`
 86: - Tutorial button lives on `#auth-screen` only — logged-in users never see it
 87: 
 88: ### Mobile UI
 89: - `syncMobileStoneDeck()` uses `stoneCounts[type]` and `stoneCapacity`. `shuffledDeck` reference was a bug (fixed: replaced with `/25`).
 90: - `updateIsMobile()` calls `initializeMobileUI()` which calls `syncMobileStoneDeck()` — triggered by `startGame()`.
 91: 
 92: ### Host ready system
 93: - Host is auto-marked ready in DB when creating room
 94: - `updateReadyButton()` hides ready button for host
 95: - `updatePlayerList()` shows Start button when `totalCount >= 2` (no longer requires `hostIsReady`)
 96: 
 97: ---
 98: 
 99: ## tutorial-mode.js — Deep Notes
100: 
101: ### Module pattern
102: IIFE assigned to `const TutorialMode`, then `window.TutorialMode = TutorialMode`. Not a class.
103: 
104: ### Step machine
105: - `STEPS[]` array, 15 entries (index 0–14)
106: - `action` field: `'read'` (button), `'click'` (spotlit element), `'move'` (position gate), `'explore'` (tile reveal gate), `'place-tile'` (tile placement gate)
107: - Blocking overlay ONLY created for `action:'click'` — all other actions leave the board interactive
108: 
109: ### Tutorial hooks (called from game-core.js)
110: | Hook | Called from | When |
111: |------|------------|------|
112: | `onTilePreReveal(tile)` | `revealTile()`, before visual | Forces `tile.shrineType='earth'` on first flip in step 3 |
113: | `onTileRevealed(tile, ss)` | `revealTile()`, after visual | Advances step 3→4 after tile flip |
114: | `onPlayerTilePlaced()` | `placeTile()`, after pawn placed | Advances step 1→2 |
115: | `onPlayerMoved(x, y)` | game-ui.js, after pawn drop | Currently no-op |
116: 
117: ### Board geometry constants
118: ```js
119: const S = 80;  // largeHexSize = TILE_SIZE * 4
120: hp(q, r) = { x: S*√3*(q+r/2), y: S*1.5*r }  // matches hexToPixel(q,r,80)
121: EARTH_POS = hp(0,0) = {x:0, y:0}      // spiral position 0
122: PLAYER_POS = hp(1,0) = {x:138.6, y:0} // NOT used for auto-placement (removed)
123: ```
124: 
125: ### Deck override
126: Set `window.tutorialDeckOverride = ['earth','catacomb','water','fire','wind','void']` BEFORE `startGame()`. `initializeDeck()` checks this and returns early after applying it. The override is nulled after use.
127: 
128: ---
129: 
130: ## Window Globals — Quick Reference
131: 
132: ```js
133: // Game state
134: window.spellSystem          // SpellSystem instance
135: window.isMultiplayer        // bool
136: window.myPlayerIndex        // int
137: window.isTutorialMode       // bool
138: window.tutorialAllowedHexes // Set<'x,y'> | null
139: window.tutorialDeckOverride // string[] | null
140: 
141: // Functions
142: window.startGame(n)         // launch local game with n players
143: window.placeTile(...)       // place a tile on the SVG board
144: window.placePlayer(x,y,c)  // place a pawn
145: window.revealTile(id)       // flip a hidden tile
146: window.addAP(n)             // add n action points (respects max)
147: window.movePlayerVisually(i,x,y,ms)  // animate pawn
148: 
149: // Modules
150: window.TutorialMode         // tutorial-mode.js public API
151: window.ScrollEffects        // scroll-effects.js namespace
152: window.SCROLL_DEFINITIONS   // all scroll metadata
153: window.SCROLL_DECKS         // scroll draw piles by element
154: window.gami                 // gamification service
155: window.crtOverlay           // CRT effect manager
156: window.emojiSystem          // emoji reactions
157: window.cosmeticsSystem      // name colour cosmetics
158: 
159: // Debug
160: window.dumpGameDebug()      // full state dump
161: window.KNOWN_BUGS           // array of bug objects
162: window.IMG_V                // cache-bust token e.g. '?v=2'
163: ```
</file>

<file path="js/scrolls/INDEX.md">
  1: # js/scrolls/ — Scroll System Index
  2: 
  3: > See parent: [js/INDEX.md](../INDEX.md) → [CLAUDE.md](../../CLAUDE.md)
  4: 
  5: ---
  6: 
  7: ## FILE MAP
  8: 
  9: | File | Responsibility |
 10: |------|---------------|
 11: | `scroll-definitions.js` | Static data: all 31 scroll names, levels, elements, stone patterns, and effect descriptions. Exposes `window.SCROLL_DEFINITIONS` and `window.SCROLL_DECKS`. |
 12: | `response-window.js` | `ResponseWindowSystem` class: detects which players can respond to a cast, manages 15s countdown, validates counter/reaction scrolls, resolves the stack. |
 13: | `effects/scroll-effects.js` | `ScrollEffects` namespace: `execute(scrollId, playerIndex, spellSystem)` for all 31 scrolls, active buff tracking, selection modes (sacrifice, tile-flip, etc.). |
 14: 
 15: ---
 16: 
 17: ## SCROLL SYSTEM FLOW
 18: 
 19: ```
 20: Player clicks "Cast Spell"
 21:     │
 22:     ▼
 23: spellSystem.activateScroll(scrollId, playerIndex)   [game-core.js]
 24:     │  validates: pattern on board, 2 AP, scroll in active/common area
 25:     │
 26:     ▼
 27: applyScrollEffects(scrollId, playerIndex, result)   [game-core.js]
 28:     │  calls ScrollEffects.execute()
 29:     │  checks result.cancelled before tracking win condition
 30:     │
 31:     ▼
 32: ScrollEffects.execute(scrollId, playerIndex, ss)    [scroll-effects.js]
 33:     │  returns { success, requiresSelection, cancelled, message }
 34:     │
 35:     ▼
 36: ResponseWindowSystem.openWindow(castData)           [response-window.js]
 37:     │  15s for other players to respond
 38:     │  on response: re-enters execute() for the counter scroll
 39:     │
 40:     ▼
 41: Win condition tracked in spellSystem.activated Set
 42: ```
 43: 
 44: ---
 45: 
 46: ## scroll-definitions.js — Deep Notes
 47: 
 48: ### Data structures
 49: ```js
 50: SCROLL_DEFINITIONS = {
 51:   EARTH_SCROLL_1: {
 52:     id: 'EARTH_SCROLL_1',
 53:     name: 'Tremor',
 54:     element: 'earth',
 55:     level: 1,
 56:     isResponse: true,       // level 1 scrolls are always response-only
 57:     patterns: [[...], [...]], // array of rotation variants
 58:     description: '...'
 59:   },
 60:   ...
 61: }
 62: 
 63: SCROLL_DECKS = {
 64:   earth: ['EARTH_SCROLL_1', ..., 'EARTH_SCROLL_5'],
 65:   water: [...],
 66:   fire:  [...],
 67:   wind:  [...],
 68:   void:  [...],
 69:   catacomb: ['CATACOMB_SCROLL_1', ..., 'CATACOMB_SCROLL_6']
 70: }
 71: ```
 72: 
 73: ### Patterns
 74: - Each pattern is a list of `{q, r, type}` relative offsets from the player's hex
 75: - Multiple variants per scroll = rotation equivalents (the system checks all)
 76: - `type` matches stone type strings: `'earth'`, `'water'`, `'fire'`, `'wind'`, `'void'`
 77: 
 78: ### Catacomb scrolls
 79: - 6 scrolls, dual-type (count toward 2 win conditions)
 80: - Drawn when revealing a catacomb tile
 81: - Stored in `SCROLL_DECKS.catacomb`
 82: 
 83: ---
 84: 
 85: ## scroll-effects.js — Deep Notes
 86: 
 87: ### execute() return contract
 88: ```js
 89: {
 90:   success: bool,            // did the effect fire?
 91:   requiresSelection: bool,  // is the system waiting for player input?
 92:   cancelled: bool,          // if true, applyScrollEffects() skips win tracking
 93:   message: string           // status bar text
 94: }
 95: ```
 96: 
 97: ### cancelled: true
 98: Used when an execute() call cannot proceed (e.g., Sacrificial Pyre with empty hand).
 99: `applyScrollEffects()` in game-core.js checks `result.cancelled` and returns before
100: tracking the element in the win condition set. Critical: the element tracking block
101: runs even if `success=false`, so `cancelled` is the only safe escape hatch.
102: 
103: ### Active buffs
104: Stored in `ScrollEffects.activeBuffs{}`. Keyed by effect name. Cleared on turn end.
105: Examples: `callToAdventure` (draw stones on tile flip), `respirate` (wind return on turn end).
106: 
107: ### Selection modes
108: Some scrolls pause normal gameplay to collect player input:
109: - `enterScrollSacrificeMode()` — player picks a scroll from hand to discard (Sacrificial Pyre)
110: - `tileMoveMode` on `window` — player picks tiles to move (Telekinesis)
111: - `takeFlightState` on `window` — player picks pawn to move (Take Flight / Breath of Power)
112: 
113: ### Scroll-specific gotchas
114: - **Sacrificial Pyre (FIRE_SCROLL_3)**: checks hand size before entering sacrifice mode. Returns `cancelled:true` if hand is empty. Previously granted fire win-con on empty hand — fixed.
115: - **Heavy Stomp (EARTH_SCROLL_3)**: calls `performTileFlip()` in scroll-effects.js (NOT `revealTile()`). Remote flips use `flipTileVisually()`. Only `revealTile()` grants +1 AP for catacomb tiles and fires tutorial hooks.
116: - **Wandering River (WATER_SCROLL_X)**: uses `getEffectiveTileElement(tile)` to override shrine type for scroll drawing. Check this before assuming `tile.shrineType` is canonical.
117: 
118: ---
119: 
120: ## response-window.js — Deep Notes
121: 
122: ### Response eligibility
123: A player can respond if:
124: 1. They have a level-1 scroll of ANY element in active area
125: 2. They have ≥ 2 AP remaining
126: 3. The response window is open (15s after cast)
127: 
128: ### Conflict resolution
129: If two players respond simultaneously, higher-rank element wins:
130: `Void > Wind > Fire > Water > Earth`
131: Same rule applies to stone conflict on the board.
132: 
133: ### Stack resolution
134: - Response resolves BEFORE the original cast takes effect
135: - A response cannot itself be responded to (no counter-counter)
</file>

<file path="js/effects-system.js">
  1: window.effectsSystem = (() => {
  2:     const EFFECTS = {
  3:         fire_effect: {
  4:             frames: 30,
  5:             fps: 18,
  6:             filter: 'none',
  7:             svgDiameter: 70,
  8:             baseFrameSize: 128,
  9:         },
 10:         ring_fire: {
 11:             frames: 30,
 12:             fps: 13,
 13:             srcId: 'fire_effect',
 14:             filter: 'hue-rotate(342deg) brightness(0.95) saturate(1.75)',
 15:             svgDiameter: 70,
 16:             baseFrameSize: 128,
 17:         }
 18:     };
 19: 
 20:     const loaded = {};
 21:     const active = [];
 22: 
 23:     const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
 24:     canvas.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;top:0;left:0;width:100%;height:100%;pointer-events:none;z-index:500;';
 25:     const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
 26: 
 27:     function resize() {
 28:         canvas.width = window.innerWidth;
 29:         canvas.height = window.innerHeight;
 30:     }
 31:     window.addEventListener('resize', resize);
 32: 
 33:     function attachCanvas() {
 34:         if (document.body) { document.body.appendChild(canvas); resize(); }
 35:         else requestAnimationFrame(attachCanvas);
 36:     }
 37:     attachCanvas();
 38: 
 39:     function preload(effectId) {
 40:         const cfg = EFFECTS[effectId];
 41:         if (!cfg || loaded[effectId]) return;
 42:         loaded[effectId] = [];
 43:         for (let i = 1; i <= cfg.frames; i++) {
 44:             const img = new Image();
 45:             img.src = `images/effects/${cfg.srcId || effectId}/${String(i).padStart(4, '0')}.png`;
 46:             loaded[effectId].push(img);
 47:         }
 48:     }
 49: 
 50:     function tileToScreen(svgX, svgY) {
 51:         const boardSvg = document.getElementById('boardSvg');
 52:         const viewport = document.getElementById('viewport');
 53:         if (!boardSvg || !viewport) return null;
 54:         const pt = boardSvg.createSVGPoint();
 55:         pt.x = svgX;
 56:         pt.y = svgY;
 57:         const ctm = viewport.getCTM();
 58:         const screenPt = pt.matrixTransform(ctm);
 59:         const svgRect = boardSvg.getBoundingClientRect();
 60:         return {
 61:             x: svgRect.left + screenPt.x,
 62:             y: svgRect.top + screenPt.y,
 63:             boardScale: Math.sqrt(ctm.a * ctm.a + ctm.b * ctm.b),
 64:         };
 65:     }
 66: 
 67:     let rafRunning = false;
 68: 
 69:     function loop() {
 70:         ctx.clearRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
 71:         const now = Date.now();
 72:         for (let i = active.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
 73:             const anim = active[i];
 74:             const cfg = EFFECTS[anim.effectId];
 75:             const frames = loaded[anim.effectId];
 76:             if (!frames) continue;
 77:             const frameIdx = Math.floor((now - anim.startTime) / (1000 / cfg.fps));
 78:             if (frameIdx >= cfg.frames) { active.splice(i, 1); continue; }
 79:             const img = frames[frameIdx];
 80:             if (!img.complete) continue;
 81:             const pos = tileToScreen(anim.svgX, anim.svgY);
 82:             if (!pos) continue;
 83:             const drawSize = cfg.svgDiameter * pos.boardScale;
 84:             ctx.save();
 85:             ctx.filter = cfg.filter;
 86:             ctx.drawImage(img, pos.x - drawSize / 2, pos.y - drawSize / 2, drawSize, drawSize);
 87:             ctx.restore();
 88:         }
 89:         if (active.length > 0) requestAnimationFrame(loop);
 90:         else rafRunning = false;
 91:     }
 92: 
 93:     Object.keys(EFFECTS).forEach(preload);
 94: 
 95:     return {
 96:         play(effectId, svgX, svgY) {
 97:             if (!EFFECTS[effectId]) return;
 98:             active.push({ effectId, svgX, svgY, startTime: Date.now() });
 99:             if (!rafRunning) { rafRunning = true; requestAnimationFrame(loop); }
100:         }
101:     };
102: })();
</file>

<file path="js/gamification-ui.js">
  1: // ============================================================
  2: // GAMIFICATION UI  (js/gamification-ui.js)
  3: // Vanilla JS modal panel: Profile · Cosmetics · Emojis · Badges · Leaderboard
  4: //
  5: // Entry point: gami_openPanel()
  6: //   Called from the "👤 Profile" button in the auth bar (index.html).
  7: // ============================================================
  8: 
  9: /** Toggle the profile panel open / closed */
 10: function gami_openPanel() {
 11:     const existing = document.getElementById('gami-panel');
 12:     if (existing) { existing.remove(); return; }
 13:     if (!window.gami?.userId) return;
 14: 
 15:     const overlay = document.createElement('div');
 16:     overlay.id        = 'gami-panel';
 17:     overlay.className = 'gami-overlay';
 18:     overlay.innerHTML = `
 19:         <div class="gami-modal" role="dialog" aria-label="Profile panel">
 20:             <div class="gami-header">
 21:                 <h2 class="gami-title">Profile</h2>
 22:                 <button class="gami-close" aria-label="Close" onclick="document.getElementById('gami-panel').remove()">×</button>
 23:             </div>
 24:             <div class="gami-tabs" role="tablist">
 25:                 <button class="gami-tab active" role="tab" onclick="gami_switchTab('profile')">Stats</button>
 26:                 <button class="gami-tab"        role="tab" onclick="gami_switchTab('cosmetics')">Colours</button>
 27:                 <button class="gami-tab"        role="tab" onclick="gami_switchTab('emojis')">Emojis</button>
 28:                 <button class="gami-tab"        role="tab" onclick="gami_switchTab('badges')">Badges</button>
 29:                 <button class="gami-tab"        role="tab" onclick="gami_switchTab('leaderboard')">Board</button>
 30:                 <button class="gami-tab"        role="tab" onclick="gami_switchTab('settings')">Settings</button>
 31:             </div>
 32:             <div id="gami-content" class="gami-content">
 33:                 <div class="gami-loading">Loading…</div>
 34:             </div>
 35:         </div>
 36:     `;
 37: 
 38:     overlay.addEventListener('click', e => {
 39:         if (e.target === overlay) overlay.remove();
 40:     });
 41: 
 42:     document.body.appendChild(overlay);
 43:     gami_switchTab('profile');
 44: }
 45: 
 46: /** Open the panel directly on the Settings tab (usable from in-game HUD) */
 47: function gami_openSettings() {
 48:     const existing = document.getElementById('gami-panel');
 49:     if (existing) {
 50:         // Panel already open — if we're already on settings, close it; otherwise switch to it
 51:         const activeTab = existing.querySelector('.gami-tab.active');
 52:         if (activeTab && activeTab.textContent.trim() === 'Settings') {
 53:             existing.remove();
 54:         } else {
 55:             gami_switchTab('settings');
 56:         }
 57:         return;
 58:     }
 59:     // Open panel, but land on settings tab instead of profile
 60:     gami_openPanel();
 61:     // gami_openPanel ends on 'profile'; immediately switch to settings
 62:     gami_switchTab('settings');
 63: }
 64: 
 65: /** Switch the active tab and load its content */
 66: async function gami_switchTab(tab) {
 67:     const panel = document.getElementById('gami-panel');
 68:     if (!panel) return;
 69: 
 70:     const tabs   = panel.querySelectorAll('.gami-tab');
 71:     const tabIdx = ['profile', 'cosmetics', 'emojis', 'badges', 'leaderboard', 'settings'].indexOf(tab);
 72:     tabs.forEach((btn, i) => btn.classList.toggle('active', i === tabIdx));
 73: 
 74:     const content = document.getElementById('gami-content');
 75:     content.innerHTML = '<div class="gami-loading">Loading…</div>';
 76: 
 77:     try {
 78:         if      (tab === 'profile')     await _renderProfile(content);
 79:         else if (tab === 'cosmetics')        _renderCosmetics(content);
 80:         else if (tab === 'emojis')           _renderEmojis(content);
 81:         else if (tab === 'badges')      await _renderBadges(content);
 82:         else if (tab === 'settings')         _renderSettings(content);
 83:         else                            await _renderLeaderboard(content);
 84:     } catch (err) {
 85:         console.error('[gami-ui] render error:', err);
 86:         content.innerHTML = '<div class="gami-loading">Failed to load. Please try again.</div>';
 87:     }
 88: }
 89: 
 90: // ── Profile tab ──────────────────────────────────────────────
 91: 
 92: async function _renderProfile(content) {
 93:     const profile = await window.gami.getProfile();
 94:     if (!profile) {
 95:         content.innerHTML = '<div class="gami-loading">Could not load profile.</div>';
 96:         return;
 97:     }
 98: 
 99:     const xpIntoLevel = profile.total_xp % 1000;
100:     const xpPercent   = Math.round(xpIntoLevel / 10);
101:     const stats       = profile.stats || {};
102:     const winRate     = stats.games_played > 0
103:         ? Math.round((stats.games_won / stats.games_played) * 100)
104:         : 0;
105:     const earnedCount = (profile.badges_earned || []).length;
106: 
107:     content.innerHTML = `
108:         <div class="gami-profile-row">
109:             <div class="gami-level-badge">
110:                 <span class="gami-level-num">${profile.current_level}</span>
111:                 <span class="gami-level-label">Level</span>
112:             </div>
113:             <div class="gami-profile-info">
114:                 <div class="gami-display-name">${_esc(profile.display_name)}</div>
115:                 <div class="gami-xp-bar-wrap">
116:                     <div class="gami-xp-bar" style="width:${xpPercent}%"></div>
117:                 </div>
118:                 <div class="gami-xp-text">${xpIntoLevel.toLocaleString()} / 1,000 XP to next level</div>
119:             </div>
120:         </div>
121: 
122:         <div class="gami-stats-grid">
123:             <div class="gami-stat">
124:                 <span class="gami-stat-val">${(profile.total_xp || 0).toLocaleString()}</span>
125:                 <span class="gami-stat-lbl">Total XP</span>
126:             </div>
127:             <div class="gami-stat">
128:                 <span class="gami-stat-val">${(profile.gold || 0).toLocaleString()}</span>
129:                 <span class="gami-stat-lbl">Gold</span>
130:             </div>
131:             <div class="gami-stat">
132:                 <span class="gami-stat-val">${stats.games_played || 0}</span>
133:                 <span class="gami-stat-lbl">Games Played</span>
134:             </div>
135:             <div class="gami-stat">
136:                 <span class="gami-stat-val">${stats.games_won || 0}</span>
137:                 <span class="gami-stat-lbl">Games Won</span>
138:             </div>
139:             <div class="gami-stat">
140:                 <span class="gami-stat-val">${winRate}%</span>
141:                 <span class="gami-stat-lbl">Win Rate</span>
142:             </div>
143:             <div class="gami-stat">
144:                 <span class="gami-stat-val">${earnedCount}</span>
145:                 <span class="gami-stat-lbl">Badges Earned</span>
146:             </div>
147:         </div>
148:     `;
149: }
150: 
151: // ── Cosmetics tab ────────────────────────────────────────────
152: 
153: function _renderCosmetics(content) {
154:     const cs = window.cosmeticsSystem;
155:     if (!cs) { content.innerHTML = '<div class="gami-loading">Cosmetics not loaded.</div>'; return; }
156: 
157:     const items    = cs.getItems();
158:     const data     = cs.getData();
159:     const equipped = data.equipped?.namecolor || null;
160:     const owned    = data.owned || [];
161:     const gold     = window.gami?.profile?.gold || 0;
162: 
163:     content.innerHTML = `
164:         <div class="gami-cos-header">
165:             <span style="color:#eee;font-size:15px;font-weight:bold;">Name Colours</span>
166:             <span style="color:#d9b08c;font-size:14px;">${gold}g</span>
167:         </div>
168:         <div class="gami-cos-list">
169:             ${items.map(item => {
170:                 const isOwned    = owned.includes(item.id);
171:                 const isEquipped = equipped === item.id;
172:                 const canAfford  = gold >= item.cost;
173: 
174:                 const previewStyle = item.value === 'rainbow'
175:                     ? 'background:linear-gradient(90deg,#f00,#f70,#ff0,#0f0,#00f,#80f);-webkit-background-clip:text;-webkit-text-fill-color:transparent;background-clip:text;animation:nameRainbow 3s linear infinite;font-weight:bold;font-size:18px;'
176:                     : `color:${item.value};font-weight:bold;font-size:18px;`;
177: 
178:                 let actionHTML;
179:                 if (isOwned) {
180:                     actionHTML = `<button class="gami-cos-btn${isEquipped ? ' equipped' : ''}"
181:                         onclick="_gami_cosmeticsEquip('${item.id}')">
182:                         ${isEquipped ? '✓ On' : 'Equip'}
183:                     </button>`;
184:                 } else {
185:                     actionHTML = `<button class="gami-cos-btn buy${canAfford ? '' : ' cant-afford'}"
186:                         onclick="_gami_cosmeticsBuy('${item.id}')">
187:                         ${item.cost}g
188:                     </button>`;
189:                 }
190: 
191:                 return `
192:                     <div class="gami-cos-item${isEquipped ? ' active' : ''}">
193:                         <div class="gami-cos-preview"><span style="${previewStyle}">Aa</span></div>
194:                         <div class="gami-cos-name">${item.name}</div>
195:                         <div class="gami-cos-action">${actionHTML}</div>
196:                     </div>`;
197:             }).join('')}
198:         </div>`;
199: }
200: 
201: // ── Emojis tab ───────────────────────────────────────────────
202: 
203: function _renderEmojis(content) {
204:     const es = window.emojiSystem;
205:     if (!es) { content.innerHTML = '<div class="gami-loading">Emoji system not loaded.</div>'; return; }
206: 
207:     es.reloadInventory();
208:     const inventory = es.getInventory();
209:     const items     = es.getItems();
210:     const tiers     = es.getTiers();
211:     const gold      = window.gami?.profile?.gold || 0;
212:     const inGame    = !!document.getElementById('game-layout')?.classList.contains('active');
213: 
214:     const owned = items.filter(e => inventory.has(e.id));
215: 
216:     let html = '<div class="gami-section-title">My Emojis</div>';
217: 
218:     if (owned.length === 0) {
219:         html += `<div style="color:#ccc;font-style:italic;padding:8px 0 16px;">No emojis yet — buy some below!</div>`;
220:     } else {
221:         html += `<div class="gami-emoji-grid">`;
222:         for (const item of owned) {
223:             const cls = 'gami-emoji-btn' + (item.isText ? ' text-emoji' : '');
224:             if (inGame) {
225:                 html += `<button class="${cls}" onclick="_gami_emojisUse('${item.id}')" title="${_esc(item.name)}">${_esc(item.display)}</button>`;
226:             } else {
227:                 html += `<div class="${cls} inactive" title="${_esc(item.name)} (join a game to use)">${_esc(item.display)}</div>`;
228:             }
229:         }
230:         html += `</div>`;
231:         if (!inGame) html += `<div style="color:#ccc;font-size:12px;padding:4px 0 14px;">Join a game to use emojis</div>`;
232:     }
233: 
234:     html += `<div class="gami-section-title" style="margin-top:4px;">Shop <span style="color:#d9b08c;float:right;">${gold}g</span></div>`;
235: 
236:     for (const tier of tiers) {
237:         const tierItems = items.filter(e => e.tier === tier.id);
238:         html += `
239:             <div class="gami-tier-section">
240:                 <div class="gami-tier-header" style="border-left-color:${tier.color};">
241:                     <span style="color:${tier.color};font-weight:bold;font-size:11px;">${tier.badge}</span>
242:                     <span style="color:#ccc;margin-left:6px;font-size:13px;">${tier.name}</span>
243:                     <span style="color:${tier.color};margin-left:auto;font-size:12px;">${tier.cost}g</span>
244:                 </div>
245:                 <div class="gami-emoji-shop-grid">`;
246:         for (const item of tierItems) {
247:             const isOwned = inventory.has(item.id);
248:             const cls = 'gami-shop-emoji' + (item.isText ? ' text-emoji' : '');
249:             if (isOwned) {
250:                 html += `
251:                     <div class="gami-shop-item owned" title="${_esc(item.name)}">
252:                         <span class="${cls}">${_esc(item.display)}</span>
253:                         <div class="gami-shop-name">${_esc(item.name)}</div>
254:                         <div class="gami-shop-owned">✓</div>
255:                     </div>`;
256:             } else {
257:                 html += `
258:                     <div class="gami-shop-item" title="${_esc(item.name)} — ${item.cost}g">
259:                         <button class="${cls}" onclick="_gami_emojisBuy('${item.id}')">${_esc(item.display)}</button>
260:                         <div class="gami-shop-name">${_esc(item.name)}</div>
261:                         <button class="gami-shop-buy-btn" style="border-color:${tier.color};color:${tier.color};"
262:                                 onclick="_gami_emojisBuy('${item.id}')">${item.cost}g</button>
263:                     </div>`;
264:             }
265:         }
266:         html += `</div></div>`;
267:     }
268: 
269:     content.innerHTML = html;
270: }
271: 
272: // ── Badges tab ───────────────────────────────────────────────
273: 
274: async function _renderBadges(content) {
275:     const badges = await window.gami.getBadgesWithStatus();
276:     if (!badges.length) {
277:         content.innerHTML = '<div class="gami-loading">No badges available.</div>';
278:         return;
279:     }
280: 
281:     const cards = badges.map(b => `
282:         <div class="gami-badge-card ${b.earned ? 'earned' : 'locked'}" title="${_esc(b.description)}">
283:             <div class="gami-badge-icon">${b.earned ? b.icon : '?'}</div>
284:             <div class="gami-badge-name">${_esc(b.name)}</div>
285:             <div class="gami-badge-desc">${_esc(b.description)}</div>
286:             ${b.gold_reward ? `<div class="gami-badge-reward">+${b.gold_reward}g</div>` : ''}
287:         </div>
288:     `).join('');
289: 
290:     content.innerHTML = `<div class="gami-badges-grid">${cards}</div>`;
291: }
292: 
293: // ── Leaderboard tab ──────────────────────────────────────────
294: 
295: async function _renderLeaderboard(content) {
296:     const rows = await window.gami.getLeaderboard(10);
297:     if (!rows.length) {
298:         content.innerHTML = '<div class="gami-loading">No players yet.</div>';
299:         return;
300:     }
301: 
302:     const medals = ['#1', '#2', '#3'];
303:     const items  = rows.map((row, i) => {
304:         const isMe = row.user_id === window.gami.userId;
305:         const rank = medals[i] || `#${i + 1}`;
306:         return `
307:             <div class="gami-lb-row ${isMe ? 'gami-lb-me' : ''}">
308:                 <span class="gami-lb-rank">${rank}</span>
309:                 <span class="gami-lb-name">${_esc(row.display_name)}</span>
310:                 <span class="gami-lb-xp">${(row.total_xp || 0).toLocaleString()} XP</span>
311:                 <span class="gami-lb-level">Lv.${row.current_level}</span>
312:             </div>
313:         `;
314:     }).join('');
315: 
316:     content.innerHTML = `<div class="gami-leaderboard">${items}</div>`;
317: }
318: 
319: // ── Main-page leaderboard widget ──────────────────────────────
320: 
321: async function loadMainLeaderboard() {
322:     const el = document.getElementById('main-leaderboard-body');
323:     if (!el) return;
324:     el.innerHTML = '<div class="gami-loading">Loading…</div>';
325: 
326:     const raw = await window.gami?.getLeaderboard(20);
327:     const rows = (raw || []).filter(r => (r.total_xp || 0) > 0).slice(0, 10);
328:     if (!rows.length) {
329:         el.innerHTML = '<div class="gami-loading" style="padding:12px;">No ranked players yet.</div>';
330:         return;
331:     }
332: 
333:     const medals = ['#1', '#2', '#3'];
334:     el.innerHTML = rows.map((row, i) => {
335:         const isMe = row.user_id === window.gami.userId;
336:         const rank = medals[i] || `#${i + 1}`;
337:         return `
338:             <div class="gami-lb-row ${isMe ? 'gami-lb-me' : ''}">
339:                 <span class="gami-lb-rank">${rank}</span>
340:                 <span class="gami-lb-name">${_esc(row.display_name)}</span>
341:                 <span class="gami-lb-xp">${(row.total_xp || 0).toLocaleString()} XP</span>
342:                 <span class="gami-lb-level">Lv.${row.current_level}</span>
343:             </div>`;
344:     }).join('');
345: }
346: 
347: window.loadMainLeaderboard = loadMainLeaderboard;

353:     gami_switchTab('cosmetics');
354: }

359: }

363:     document.getElementById('gami-panel')?.remove();
364: }

367:     await window.emojiSystem.purchaseEmoji(id);
368:     gami_switchTab('emojis');
