6597: 
6598:         function updateAllWaterStoneVisuals() {
6599:             // Update all water stones to show what they're mimicking
6600:             placedStones.forEach(stone => {
6601:                 if (stone.type === 'water') {
6602:                     updateWaterStoneVisual(stone);
6603:                 }
6604:             });
6605:         }
6606: 
6607:         function updateAllVoidNullificationVisuals() {
6608:             // Update all stones to show if they're nullified by void
6609:             placedStones.forEach(stone => {
6610:                 // Remove any existing nullification indicator
6611:                 const existingNullIndicator = stone.element.querySelector('.void-nullification-indicator');
6612:                 if (existingNullIndicator) {
6613:                     existingNullIndicator.remove();
6614:                 }
6615: 
6616:                 // Check if this stone is nullified by adjacent void
6617:                 // (Only fire, wind, and earth have abilities that can be nullified)
6618:                 if (stone.type === 'fire' || stone.type === 'wind' || stone.type === 'earth') {
6619:                     const hasVoid = hasAdjacentStoneType(stone.x, stone.y, 'void');
6620:                     if (hasVoid) {
6621:                         // Add nullification indicator (X or crossed-out effect)
6622:                         const nullIndicator = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'circle');
6623:                         nullIndicator.setAttribute('cx', 0);
6624:                         nullIndicator.setAttribute('cy', 0);
6625:                         nullIndicator.setAttribute('r', STONE_SIZE + 3);
6626:                         nullIndicator.setAttribute('class', 'void-nullification-indicator');
6627:                         nullIndicator.setAttribute('fill', 'none');
6628:                         nullIndicator.setAttribute('stroke', STONE_TYPES['void'].color);
6629:                         nullIndicator.setAttribute('stroke-width', '2');
6630:                         nullIndicator.setAttribute('stroke-dasharray', '2,2');
6631:                         nullIndicator.setAttribute('opacity', '0.6');
6632: 
6633:                         // Insert before other elements
6634:                         stone.element.insertBefore(nullIndicator, stone.element.firstChild);
6635: 
6636:                         console.log(`✨ ${stone.type} at (${stone.x.toFixed(1)}, ${stone.y.toFixed(1)}) is nullified by void`);
6637:                     }
6638:                 }
6639:             });
6640:         }
6641: 
6642:         function updateWaterStoneVisual(waterStone) {
6643:             const effectiveType = getEffectiveStoneType(waterStone);
6644:             const chainedAbility = getChainedAbility(waterStone.x, waterStone.y);
6645: 
6646:             // Remove any existing indicators
6647:             const existingIndicator = waterStone.element.querySelector('.mimicry-indicator');
6648:             if (existingIndicator) {
6649:                 existingIndicator.remove();
6650:             }
6651:             const existingChainIndicator = waterStone.element.querySelector('.chain-indicator');
6652:             if (existingChainIndicator) {
6653:                 existingChainIndicator.remove();
6654:             }
6655: 
6656:             // Determine what to show: chained ability takes precedence over mimicry
6657:             // because chaining uses wind-outranks-earth logic
6658:             const displayAbility = chainedAbility || (effectiveType !== 'water' ? effectiveType : null);
6659: 
6660:             if (displayAbility) {
6661:                 const isChained = !!chainedAbility;
6662:                 const indicator = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'circle');
6663:                 indicator.setAttribute('cx', 0);
6664:                 indicator.setAttribute('cy', 0);
6665:                 indicator.setAttribute('r', STONE_SIZE + 3);
6666:                 indicator.setAttribute('class', isChained ? 'chain-indicator' : 'mimicry-indicator');
6667:                 indicator.setAttribute('fill', 'none');
6668:                 indicator.setAttribute('stroke', STONE_TYPES[displayAbility].color);
6669:                 indicator.setAttribute('stroke-width', '2');
6670:                 indicator.setAttribute('stroke-dasharray', isChained ? '5,2' : '3,3');
6671:                 if (isChained) {
6672:                     indicator.setAttribute('opacity', '0.7');
6673:                 }
6674: 
6675:                 // Insert before other elements
6676:                 waterStone.element.insertBefore(indicator, waterStone.element.firstChild);
6677: 
6678:                 if (isChained) {
6679:                     console.log(`💧 Water at (${waterStone.x.toFixed(1)}, ${waterStone.y.toFixed(1)}) has chained ${displayAbility} ability`);
6680:                 } else {
6681:                     console.log(`💧 Water at (${waterStone.x.toFixed(1)}, ${waterStone.y.toFixed(1)}) is mimicking ${displayAbility}`);
6682:                 }
6683:             }
6684:         }
6685: 
6686:         function getNeighborStones(x, y) {
6687:             const neighbors = [];
6688:             const hexPositions = getAllHexagonPositions();
6689: 
6690:             hexPositions.forEach(pos => {
6691:                 const dist = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(x - pos.x, 2) + Math.pow(y - pos.y, 2));
6692:                 if (dist > 5 && dist < TILE_SIZE * 2.5) {
6693:                     const stone = placedStones.find(s => {
6694:                         const stoneDist = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(s.x - pos.x, 2) + Math.pow(s.y - pos.y, 2));
6695:                         return stoneDist < 5;
6696:                     });
6697:                     if (stone) {
6698:                         neighbors.push(stone);
6699:                     }
6700:                 }
6701:             });
6702: 
6703:             return neighbors;
6704:         }
6705: 
6706:         function hasAdjacentStoneType(x, y, type) {
6707:             const neighbors = getNeighborStones(x, y);
6708:             return neighbors.some(s => s.type === type);
6709:         }
6710: 
6711:         // Stone rank for water mimicry priority (lower = higher priority)
6712:         const STONE_RANK = {
6713:             'void': 1,
6714:             'wind': 2,
6715:             'fire': 3,
6716:             'water': 4,
6717:             'earth': 5
6718:         };
6719: 
6720:         // Get the effective type of a stone (considering water mimicry)
6721:         function getEffectiveStoneType(stone) {
6722:             if (stone.type !== 'water') {
6723:                 return stone.type;
6724:             }
6725: 
6726:             // Water mimics adjacent stones
6727:             const neighbors = getNeighborStones(stone.x, stone.y);
6728:             if (neighbors.length === 0) {
6729:                 return 'water'; // No neighbors, just water
6730:             }
6731: 
6732:             // Find highest-ranked adjacent stone (lowest rank number)
6733:             let bestRank = Infinity;
6734:             let mimicType = 'water';
6735: 
6736:             neighbors.forEach(neighbor => {
6737:                 const rank = STONE_RANK[neighbor.type] || 999;
6738:                 if (rank < bestRank && neighbor.type !== 'water') {
6739:                     bestRank = rank;
6740:                     mimicType = neighbor.type;
6741:                 }
6742:             });
6743: 
6744:             return mimicType;
6745:         }
6746: 
6747:         function recheckAllStoneInteractions() {
6748:             // Re-check interactions for all fire stones on the board
6749:             // This ensures fire destroys adjacent stones when void is removed
6750:             const fireStones = placedStones.filter(s => s.type === 'fire');
6751: 
6752:             fireStones.forEach(stone => {
6753:                 const fireHasVoid = hasAdjacentStoneType(stone.x, stone.y, 'void');
6754:                 if (!fireHasVoid) {
6755:                     // Fire is not nullified, check what it should destroy
6756:                     const neighbors = getNeighborStones(stone.x, stone.y);
6757:                     const stonesToDestroy = [];
6758:                     neighbors.forEach(neighbor => {
6759:                         // Fire destroys ALL stones except void and fire
6760:                         // (Water is NOT protected - fire destroys water)
6761:                         if (neighbor.type !== 'void' && neighbor.type !== 'fire') {
6762:                             stonesToDestroy.push(neighbor.id);
6763:                         }
6764:                     });
6765: 
6766:                     // Destroy adjacent stones (using direct removal to avoid recursion)
6767:                     stonesToDestroy.forEach(targetId => {
6768:                         const target = placedStones.find(s => s.id === targetId);
6769:                         if (target) {
6770:                             window.effectsSystem?.play('fire_effect', target.x, target.y);
6771:                             target.element.remove();
6772:                             placedStones = placedStones.filter(s => s.id !== targetId);
6773:                             returnStoneToPool(target.type);
6774:                             updateStatus(`Fire destroyed ${target.type} stone!`);
6775:                         }
6776:                     });
6777:                 }
6778:             });
6779:         }
6780: 
6781:         function processStoneInteractions(x, y, type) {
6782:             const neighbors = getNeighborStones(x, y);
6783: 
6784:             // Special case: water stone placed with both fire and a higher-priority stone
6785:             // Water mimics the higher-priority stone, then gets destroyed by fire
6786:             if (type === 'water') {
6787:                 const hasActiveFire = neighbors.some(n => n.type === 'fire' && !hasAdjacentStoneType(n.x, n.y, 'void'));
6788:                 if (hasActiveFire) {
6789:                     // Check what the water would mimic
6790:                     const effectiveType = getEffectiveStoneType({ x, y, type: 'water' });
6791:                     // If water is mimicking something other than water or fire, it gets destroyed
6792:                     if (effectiveType !== 'water' && effectiveType !== 'fire') {
6793:                         const stoneToRemove = placedStones.find(s => s.x === x && s.y === y);
6794:                         if (stoneToRemove) {
6795:                             setTimeout(() => {
6796:                                 removeStone(stoneToRemove.id);
6797:                                 updateStatus(`Water mimicked ${effectiveType}, then was destroyed by fire!`);
6798:                             }, 100);
6799:                         }
6800:                         return; // Stop processing other interactions
6801:                     }
6802:                 }
6803:             }
6804: 
6805:             // Fire destroys adjacent non-void, non-fire stones
6806:             // But only if fire itself is not nullified by an adjacent void
6807:             if (type === 'fire') {
6808:                 const fireHasVoid = hasAdjacentStoneType(x, y, 'void');
6809: 
6810:                 if (!fireHasVoid) {
6811:                     const stonesToDestroy = [];
6812:                     neighbors.forEach(neighbor => {
6813:                         // Don't destroy void or fire
6814:                         // Fire destroys ALL other stones, even if they're voided
6815:                         if (neighbor.type !== 'void' && neighbor.type !== 'fire') {
6816:                             stonesToDestroy.push(neighbor);
6817:                         }
6818:                     });
6819: 
6820:                     // Destroy all adjacent stones (voided or not)
6821:                     stonesToDestroy.forEach(stone => {
6822:                         window.effectsSystem?.play('fire_effect', stone.x, stone.y);
6823:                         removeStone(stone.id);
6824:                         updateStatus(`Fire destroyed ${stone.type} stone!`);
6825:                     });
6826:                 }
6827:             }
6828: 
6829:             // Fire also checks incoming threats
6830:             neighbors.forEach(neighbor => {
6831:                 if (neighbor.type === 'fire' && type !== 'void' && type !== 'fire') {
6832:                     const hasVoid = hasAdjacentStoneType(neighbor.x, neighbor.y, 'void');
6833:                     if (!hasVoid) {
6834:                         const stoneToRemove = placedStones.find(s => s.x === x && s.y === y);
6835:                         if (stoneToRemove) {
6836:                             window.effectsSystem?.play('fire_effect', x, y);
6837:                             removeStone(stoneToRemove.id);
6838:                             updateStatus(`Fire destroyed ${type} stone!`);
6839:                         }
6840:                     }
6841:                 }
6842:             });
6843:         }
6844: 
6845:         function removeStone(stoneId) {
6846:             const stone = placedStones.find(s => s.id === stoneId);
6847:             if (stone) {
6848:                 stone.element.remove();
6849:                 placedStones = placedStones.filter(s => s.id !== stoneId);
6850:                 returnStoneToPool(stone.type);
6851:                 updateTileClasses();
6852: 
6853:                 // Re-check all fire stones to see if they should activate
6854:                 recheckAllStoneInteractions();
6855: 
6856:                 // Update all water stone visuals since chaining may have changed
6857:                 updateAllWaterStoneVisuals();
6858: 
6859:                 // Update void nullification indicators
6860:                 updateAllVoidNullificationVisuals();
6861:             }
6862:         }
6863: 
6864:         
6865:         function isHexOccupiedByOtherPlayer(x, y) {
6866:             if (!Array.isArray(playerPositions) || typeof activePlayerIndex !== 'number') return false;
6867:             return playerPositions.some((p, idx) => {
6868:                 if (!p) return false;
6869:                 if (idx === activePlayerIndex) return false; // ignore self
6870:                 const dist = Math.hypot(p.x - x, p.y - y);
6871:                 return dist < 5; // same positional threshold used elsewhere
6872:             });
6873:         }
6874: 
6875:         function canPlayerMoveToHex(x, y, logBlocked = false) {
6876:             // Block movement onto hexes occupied by other players (prevents moving "through" players as pathing is step-wise)
6877:             if (isHexOccupiedByOtherPlayer(x, y)) {
6878:                 if (logBlocked) console.log(`❌ Cannot move to (${x.toFixed(1)}, ${y.toFixed(1)}): occupied by another player`);
6879:                 return { canMove: false, cost: Infinity };
6880:             }
6881: 
6882:             const stone = placedStones.find(s => {
6883:                 const dist = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(s.x - x, 2) + Math.pow(s.y - y, 2));
6884:                 return dist < 5;
6885:             });
6886: 
6887:             if (!stone) return { canMove: true, cost: 1 };
6888: 
6889:             // Mudslide buff: earth and water stones act as wind stones (free movement)
6890:             const mudslideBuff = spellSystem?.scrollEffects?.activeBuffs?.mudslide;
6891:             if (mudslideBuff && mudslideBuff.playerIndex === activePlayerIndex) {
6892:                 if (stone.type === 'earth' || stone.type === 'water') {
6893:                     // Treat as wind stone — free movement, unless nullified by void
6894:                     const hasVoid = hasAdjacentStoneType(x, y, 'void');
6895:                     if (hasVoid) return { canMove: true, cost: 1 };
6896:                     console.log(`🌊 Mudslide: ${stone.type} stone at (${x.toFixed(1)}, ${y.toFixed(1)}) acts as wind (free movement)`);
6897:                     return { canMove: true, cost: 0 };
6898:                 }
6899:             }
6900: 
6901:             // Handle water stones with chaining
6902:             if (stone.type === 'water') {
6903:                 const chainedAbility = getChainedAbility(x, y);
6904:                 console.log(`💧 Water at (${x.toFixed(1)}, ${y.toFixed(1)}) has chained ability: ${chainedAbility || 'none'}`);
6905: 
6906:                 if (chainedAbility === 'wind') {
6907:                     // Wind chains through water - free movement
6908:                     console.log(`✓ Wind chaining active - water becomes free movement`);
6909:                     return { canMove: true, cost: 0 };
6910:                 } else if (chainedAbility === 'earth') {
6911:                     // Earth chains through water - blocks movement
6912:                     console.log(`✓ Earth chaining active - water blocks movement`);
6913:                     if (logBlocked) console.log(`❌ Cannot move to (${x.toFixed(1)}, ${y.toFixed(1)}): Water has chained Earth ability (blocks movement)`);
6914:                     return { canMove: false, cost: Infinity };
6915:                 }
6916: 
6917:                 // No chaining effects, normal water cost
6918:                 console.log(`💧 No chaining - normal water cost (2 AP)`);
6919:                 return { canMove: true, cost: 2 };
6920:             }
6921: 
6922:             // Handle non-water stones based on their ACTUAL type, not mimicry
6923:             // (Mimicry is visual only, doesn't affect movement)
6924: 
6925:             // Earth blocks movement (unless nullified by void)
6926:             if (stone.type === 'earth') {
6927:                 const hasVoid = hasAdjacentStoneType(x, y, 'void');
6928:                 if (hasVoid) return { canMove: true, cost: 1 }; // Nullified by void, reverts to baseline
6929:                 if (logBlocked) console.log(`❌ Cannot move to (${x.toFixed(1)}, ${y.toFixed(1)}): Earth stone blocks movement (needs adjacent Void to nullify)`);
6930:                 return { canMove: false, cost: Infinity };
6931:             }
6932: 
6933:             // Wind is free (0 cost) - ability overrides baseline
6934:             if (stone.type === 'wind') {
6935:                 const hasVoid = hasAdjacentStoneType(x, y, 'void');
6936:                 if (hasVoid) return { canMove: true, cost: 1 }; // Nullified by void, reverts to baseline
6937:                 return { canMove: true, cost: 0 }; // Wind ability: free movement
6938:             }
6939: 
6940:             // All other stones (void, fire, etc.) cost 1 AP (baseline)
6941:             return { canMove: true, cost: 1 };
6942:         }
6943: 
6944:         function startStoneDrag(stoneId, e) {
6945:             // Check if it's this player's turn and no pending cascade
6946:             if (!canTakeAction()) {
6947:                 notYourTurn();
6948:                 return;
6949:             }
6950: 
6951:             const stone = placedStones.find(s => s.id === stoneId);
6952:             if (!stone) {
6953:                 console.log('❌ Stone not found for dragging');
6954:                 return;
6955:             }
6956: 
6957:             // Check if stone is adjacent to player
6958:             if (!playerPosition) {
6959:                 updateStatus('No player on board!');
6960:                 return;
6961:             }
6962: 
6963:             if (!isAdjacentToPlayer(stone.x, stone.y)) {
6964:                 updateStatus('Can only move stones adjacent to player!');
6965:                 console.log(`❌ Cannot drag stone id=${stoneId}: not adjacent to player`);
6966:                 return;
6967:             }
6968: 
6969:             console.log(`✅ Starting drag for stone id=${stoneId}, type=${stone.type} (adjacent to player)`);
6970: 
6971:             // Clean up any pre-existing ghost (prevents orphaned stamps)
6972:             if (ghostStone) {
6973:                 ghostStone.remove();
6974:                 ghostStone = null;
6975:             }
6976: 
6977:             isDraggingStone = true;
6978:             draggedStoneId = stoneId;
6979:             draggedStoneType = stone.type;
6980:             draggedStoneOriginalPos = { x: stone.x, y: stone.y };
6981:             stone.element.remove();
6982:             placedStones = placedStones.filter(s => s.id !== stoneId);
6983:             updateTileClasses();
6984: 
6985:             // Re-check all fire stones to see if they should activate/deactivate
6986:             recheckAllStoneInteractions();
6987: 
6988:             // Update all water stone visuals since chaining may have changed
6989:             updateAllWaterStoneVisuals();
6990: 
6991:             // Update void nullification indicators
6992:             updateAllVoidNullificationVisuals();
6993: 
6994:             const rect = boardSvg.getBoundingClientRect();
6995:             const screenX = e.clientX - rect.left;
6996:             const screenY = e.clientY - rect.top;
6997:             const world = screenToWorld(screenX, screenY);
6998: 
6999:             ghostStone = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'g');
7000:             ghostStone.setAttribute('class', 'stone stone-ghost');
7001:             ghostStone.setAttribute('transform', `translate(${world.x}, ${world.y})`);
7002: 
7003:             const circle = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'circle');
7004:             circle.setAttribute('cx', 0);
7005:             circle.setAttribute('cy', 0);
7006:             circle.setAttribute('r', STONE_SIZE);
7007:             circle.setAttribute('class', 'stone-piece');
7008:             circle.setAttribute('fill', darkenHex(STONE_TYPES[draggedStoneType].color, 0.55));
7009:             circle.setAttribute('stroke', STONE_TYPES[draggedStoneType].color);
7010:             circle.setAttribute('stroke-width', '1.5');
7011: 
7012:             const ghostImg = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'image');
7013:             ghostImg.setAttribute('href', STONE_TYPES[draggedStoneType].img);
7014:             ghostImg.setAttribute('x', -STONE_SIZE);
7015:             ghostImg.setAttribute('y', -STONE_SIZE);
7016:             ghostImg.setAttribute('width', STONE_SIZE * 2);
7017:             ghostImg.setAttribute('height', STONE_SIZE * 2);
7018:             // No opacity reduction — symbol renders at full strength
7019: 
7020:             ghostStone.appendChild(circle);
7021:             ghostStone.appendChild(ghostImg);
7022:             viewport.appendChild(ghostStone);
7023:         }
7024: 
7025:         function updateStoneCount(type) {
7026:             // Update old UI
7027:             const oldEl = document.getElementById(type + '-count');
7028:             if (oldEl) oldEl.textContent = stoneCounts[type] + '/' + stoneCapacity[type];
7029:             updateSourceCount(type);
7030: 
7031:             // Update new UI
7032:             const newCountEl = document.getElementById('new-' + type + '-count');
7033:             if (newCountEl) newCountEl.textContent = stoneCounts[type] + '/' + stoneCapacity[type];
7034:             const newSourceEl = document.getElementById('new-' + type + '-source');
7035:             if (newSourceEl) newSourceEl.textContent = sourcePool[type] + '/' + (sourcePoolCapacity[type] ?? 25);
7036: 
7037:             // Refresh Players panel so self-card stone counts stay in sync
7038:             if (typeof updateOpponentPanel === 'function') updateOpponentPanel();
7039: 
7040:             // Update void AP whenever void stones change
7041:             if (type === 'void') {
7042:                 // If Transmute is open, suppress void AP auto-sync for that player
7043:                 const displayIndex = (typeof isMultiplayer !== 'undefined' && isMultiplayer && myPlayerIndex !== null)
7044:                     ? myPlayerIndex : activePlayerIndex;
7045:                 const suppress = spellSystem?.scrollEffects?.activeBuffs?.suppressVoidAPSync;
7046:                 if (suppress && suppress.playerIndex === displayIndex) {
7047:                     console.log(`💨 Void stones changed to ${playerPool.void}, suppressing void AP sync`);
7048:                 } else {
7049:                     console.log(`💨 Void stones changed to ${playerPool.void}, updating void AP to match`);
7050:                     updateVoidAP();
7051:                 }
7052:             }
7053:         }
7054: 
7055:         // Expose updateStoneCount for scroll effects system
7056:         window.updateStoneCount = updateStoneCount;
7057: 
7058:         // Reset all per-game state so a new game starts clean (no leftover resources)
7059:         function resetGameResources() {
7060:             // Remove any leftover game-over overlays from a previous game
7061:             document.querySelectorAll('.game-over-overlay').forEach(el => el.remove());
7062: 
7063:             // Remove all stone SVG elements still on the board from the previous game
7064:             document.querySelectorAll('.stone').forEach(el => el.remove());
7065:             placedStones.length = 0;
7066: 
7067:             // Stone pools
7068:             playerPools.length = 0;
7069:             sourcePool.earth = 20;
7070:             sourcePool.water = 20;
7071:             sourcePool.fire  = 20;
7072:             sourcePool.wind  = 20;
7073:             sourcePool.void  = 20;
7074: 
7075:             // AP
7076:             currentAP = 5;
7077:             voidAP    = 0;
7078:             playerAPs.length = 0;
7079: 
7080:             // Scroll state (hands, activated elements)
7081:             spellSystem.playerScrolls = [];
7082: 
7083:             // Reset common area (clear stale cards from previous game)
7084:             spellSystem.commonArea = { earth: null, water: null, fire: null, wind: null, void: null, catacomb: null };
7085: 
7086:             // Reset scroll decks to full from definitions (or fallback)
7087:             if (typeof SCROLL_DECKS !== 'undefined') {
7088:                 spellSystem.scrollDecks = {};
7089:                 Object.keys(SCROLL_DECKS).forEach(element => {
7090:                     spellSystem.scrollDecks[element] = [...SCROLL_DECKS[element]];
7091:                 });
7092:             } else {
7093:                 spellSystem.scrollDecks = {
7094:                     earth: ['EARTH_SCROLL_1','EARTH_SCROLL_2','EARTH_SCROLL_3','EARTH_SCROLL_4','EARTH_SCROLL_5'],
7095:                     water: ['WATER_SCROLL_1','WATER_SCROLL_2','WATER_SCROLL_3','WATER_SCROLL_4','WATER_SCROLL_5'],
7096:                     fire:  ['FIRE_SCROLL_1','FIRE_SCROLL_2','FIRE_SCROLL_3','FIRE_SCROLL_4','FIRE_SCROLL_5'],
7097:                     wind:  ['WIND_SCROLL_1','WIND_SCROLL_2','WIND_SCROLL_3','WIND_SCROLL_4','WIND_SCROLL_5'],
7098:                     void:  ['VOID_SCROLL_1','VOID_SCROLL_2','VOID_SCROLL_3','VOID_SCROLL_4','VOID_SCROLL_5'],
7099:                     catacomb: ['CATACOMB_SCROLL_1','CATACOMB_SCROLL_2','CATACOMB_SCROLL_3','CATACOMB_SCROLL_4','CATACOMB_SCROLL_5']
7100:                 };
7101:             }
7102:             spellSystem.shuffleAllDecks();
7103: 
7104:             // Scroll effect buffs and interactive-scroll states
7105:             if (spellSystem.scrollEffects) {
7106:                 spellSystem.scrollEffects.activeBuffs = {};
7107:             }
7108:             window.telekinesisState = null;
7109:             window.takeFlightState  = null;
7110: 
7111:             console.log('🔄 Game resources reset (scroll decks and common area cleared)');
7112:         }
7113:         window.resetGameResources = resetGameResources;
</file>

<file path="js/tutorial-mode.js">
  1: /**
  2:  * Tutorial Mode for Godaigo — Interactive Guided Tutorial
  3:  *
  4:  * Features:
  5:  *  - Auto-builds a scripted board (earth tile at center, player + enemy pawns placed)
  6:  *  - 14-step walkthrough using the official voice-recorded transcript
  7:  *  - Spotlight system: dims everything and highlights one UI element at a time
  8:  *  - Movement gating: restricts the pawn to the tutorial destination
  9:  *  - Click-to-advance: certain steps wait for the player to click the spotlit element
 10:  *
 11:  * Entry point: TutorialMode.start()  (called from the auth-screen button)
 12:  * Hooks required in game code:
 13:  *   game-core.js → window.placeTile, window.spellSystem, tutorialDeckOverride in initializeDeck,
 14:  *                  TutorialMode.onTileRevealed in revealTile
 15:  *   game-ui.js   → tutorialBlocked gate + TutorialMode.onPlayerMoved after placePlayer
 16:  */
 17: 
 18: window.isTutorialMode      = false;
 19: window.tutorialAllowedHexes = null;   // Set<'x,y'>: restricts pawn drops when non-null
 20: 
 21: const TutorialMode = (function () {
 22: 
 23:     // ── Board geometry (TILE_SIZE=20, largeHexSize=80) ────────────────────────
 24:     const S = 80, SQ3 = Math.sqrt(3);
 25:     const hp = (q, r) => ({ x: S * SQ3 * (q + r / 2), y: S * 1.5 * r });
 26: 
 27:     const EARTH_POS  = hp( 0,  0);  // center of spiral → earth tile
 28:     const PLAYER_POS = hp( 1,  0);  // east  → player pawn start
 29:     const ENEMY_POS  = hp(-1,  2);  // south → opponent pawn (cosmetic only)
 30: 
 31:     const TUTORIAL_DECK = ['earth', 'catacomb', 'water', 'fire', 'wind', 'void'];
 32: 
 33:     // ── State ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 34:     let currentStep       = -1;
 35:     let boardRingEl       = null;   // SVG pulsing ring on earth tile
 36:     let modalEl           = null;
 37:     let overlayEl         = null;   // blocking dim overlay
 38:     let spotlightEl       = null;   // currently spotlit DOM element
 39:     let spotlightHandler  = null;   // {el, fn} for click-to-advance cleanup
 40:     let earthRevealed     = false;  // tracks whether the first tile reveal has been processed
 41:     let exitBtnEl         = null;   // persistent exit button shown for the whole tutorial
 42:     let liftedAncestors   = [];     // ancestors temporarily raised above the overlay
 43: 
 44:     // ── New kinesthetic state ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
 45:     let patternPollInterval  = null;   // setInterval for checkPattern polling
 46:     let hintTimer            = null;   // setTimeout for 15-second inactivity hint
 47: 
 48:     // ── Step definitions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 49:     // action:
 50:     //   'read'  – Next/Continue button advances
 51:     //   'move'  – onPlayerMoved() fires advance (pawn reaches earth tile)
 52:     //   'click' – clicking the spotlit element fires advance
 53:     //
 54:     // spotlight: CSS selector of HTML element to highlight (null = board ring only)
 55:     // boardRing: true = show pulsing ring on earth tile
 56:     // modalPos:  'center' (default) | 'corner' (bottom-right, used when spotlight is active)
 57:     const STEPS = [
 58:         // ── 0  welcome (read — only intro step permitted as read-only) ─────────
 59:         {
 60:             id: 'welcome',
 61:             title: 'Welcome to Godaigo!',
 62:             content: `Hi, welcome to the tutorial for <strong>Godaigo: Secret of the Five Elements</strong>. Thanks for playing!
 63:             <div style="margin-top:12px;">
 64:                 Your goal is to <strong>master all 5 elements</strong> by finding and activating one scroll of each type.
 65:             </div>
 66:             <div style="margin-top:14px; display:flex; justify-content:center; gap:14px; font-size:18px; flex-wrap:wrap; line-height:2.2;">
 67:                 <span style="color:#69d83a;">⬡ Earth</span>
 68:                 <span style="color:#5894f4;">⬡ Water</span>
 69:                 <span style="color:#ed1b43;">⬡ Fire</span>
 70:                 <span style="color:#ffce00;">⬡ Wind</span>
 71:                 <span style="color:#9458f4;">⬡ Void</span>
 72:             </div>
 73:             <div style="margin-top:10px; color:#aaa; font-size:13px;">
 74:                 Be the first player to activate all five to win — and escape the mystical island!
 75:             </div>`,
 76:             action: 'read',
 77:             nextLabel: "Let's Go!",
 78:             modalPos: 'center'
 79:         },
 80:         // ── 1  place tile (action-gated: onPlayerTilePlaced) ──────────────────
 81:         {
 82:             id: 'tile-placed',
 83:             title: 'Place Your Starting Tile',
 84:             content: `See the <strong>hexagonal tile in the left panel?</strong> That's your Player Tile.
 85:             <div style="margin-top:10px;">
 86:                 <strong>Drag it onto the board</strong> and snap it next to the edge of two existing tiles. You'll see a ghost tile showing where it'll land.
 87:             </div>`,
 88:             action: 'place-tile',
 89:             nextLabel: null,
 90:             spotlight: '#new-player-tile-deck',
 91:             modalPos: 'corner'
 92:         },
 93:         // ── 2  camera (brief read — no sensible action gate for controls intro) ─
 94:         {
 95:             id: 'camera',
 96:             title: 'Camera Controls',
 97:             content: `A few handy controls before you start moving:
 98:             <ul style="margin:10px 0; padding-left:18px; line-height:2;">
 99:                 <li><strong>Scroll wheel</strong> — zoom in / out</li>
100:                 <li><strong>Right-click drag</strong> — pan the board</li>
101:                 <li><strong>Right-click drag outside tiles</strong> — rotate the board</li>
102:             </ul>
103:             <div style="color:#aaa; font-size:13px;">
104:                 Try zooming now — it never costs AP.
105:             </div>`,
106:             action: 'read',
107:             nextLabel: 'Got it'
108:         },
109:         // ── 3  explore / flip tile (action-gated: onTileRevealed) ────────────
110:         {
111:             id: 'move-pawn',
112:             title: 'Explore the Board',
113:             content: `<strong>Drag your pawn</strong> to move it across the board.
114:             <div style="margin-top:10px;">
115:                 Each hex costs <strong>1 Action Point</strong>. You start every turn with 5 AP.
116:             </div>
117:             <div style="margin-top:10px;">
118:                 All the tiles are hidden — <strong>step onto any face-down tile to flip it</strong> and reveal the shrine underneath!
119:             </div>`,
120:             action: 'explore',
121:             nextLabel: null,
122:             spotlight: '#hud-ap-value',
123:             modalPos: 'corner'
124:         },
125:         // ── 4  scroll found (read-only) ───────────────────────────────────────
126:         {
127:             id: 'scroll-found',
128:             title: 'You Found a Scroll!',
129:             content: `When you flip a tile, a scroll is added to your <strong>hand</strong>. You got an <strong style="color:#69d83a;">Avalanche (Earth V)</strong> scroll — a powerful earth spell.
130:             <div style="margin-top:10px;">
131:                 We'll use it soon. For now, let's collect some <strong style="color:#69d83a;">Earth stones</strong> to power it up.
132:             </div>`,
133:             action: 'read',
134:             nextLabel: 'Got it!',
135:             modalPos: 'corner'
136:         },
137:         // ── 5  earth shrine (action-gated: onEndTurn at EARTH_POS) ───────────
138:         {
139:             id: 'earth-shrine',
140:             title: 'Collect Earth Stones',
141:             content: `You flipped an <strong style="color:#69d83a;">Earth tile</strong>! The glowing center is the <strong>Earth shrine</strong>.
142:             <div style="margin-top:10px;">
143:                 <strong>Walk your pawn to the glowing center</strong>, then click <strong>End Turn</strong> to collect <strong style="color:#69d83a;">5 Earth stones</strong>.
144:             </div>
145:             <div style="margin-top:10px; color:#aaa; font-size:13px;">
146:                 Stones come from shared <strong>Source Pools</strong> — 25 of each type max.
147:             </div>`,
148:             action: 'end-turn',
149:             nextLabel: null,
150:             boardRing: true,
151:             freeMove: true,
152:             spotlight: '#end-turn',
153:             modalPos: 'corner'
154:         },
155:         // ── 6  open scrolls hand panel (action-gated: click) ─────────────────
156:         {
157:             id: 'open-scrolls',
158:             title: 'Open Your Hand',
159:             content: `Click the <strong>Hand</strong> button in the dock to open your scroll hand panel.`,
160:             action: 'click',
161:             spotlight: '#panel-btn-hand',
162:             nextLabel: null,
163:             modalPos: 'corner'
164:         },
165:         // ── 7  move scroll to active area (action-gated: onScrollMoved hand→active) ─
166:         {
167:             id: 'scrolls-explained',
168:             title: 'Move Scroll to Active Area',
169:             content: `Your scrolls are split across three panels:
170:             <ul style="margin:10px 0; padding-left:18px; line-height:2.1;">
171:                 <li><strong>Hand</strong> — private; max 2 scrolls</li>
172:                 <li><strong>Active</strong> — face-up, visible to all; scrolls here can be cast</li>
173:                 <li><strong>Common Area</strong> — shared pool any player can cast from</li>
174:             </ul>
175:             <div style="margin-top:8px;">
176:                 <strong>Click "Move to Active Area"</strong> on your Avalanche scroll to get it ready to cast.
177:             </div>`,
178:             action: 'scroll-moved',
179:             nextLabel: null,
180:             modalPos: 'corner'
181:         },

187:             <div style="margin-top:10px;">
188:                 To cast a scroll you need to:
189:                 <ol style="margin:8px 0; padding-left:18px; line-height:2;">
190:                     <li>Have it in your <strong>Active Area</strong></li>

194:                 </ol>
195:             </div>`,
196:             action: 'read',
197:             nextLabel: "Let's try it!"
198:         },

204:             <div style="margin-top:10px;">

206:                 Placing stones is free — no AP cost.
207:             </div>`,

209:             stoneType: 'earth',

347:             title: "You're Ready!",
348:             content: `That's the basics of Godaigo!
349:             <div style="margin-top:12px;">
350:                 Keep exploring — flip hidden tiles, collect scrolls, build stone patterns, and activate one scroll of each element to win.
351:             </div>
352:             <div style="margin-top:10px; color:#aaa; font-size:13px;">

358:     ];
359: 
360:     // ── Board setup ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
361: 
362:     function start() {
363:         window.isTutorialMode       = true;
364:         window.tutorialDeckOverride = [...TUTORIAL_DECK];
365:         earthRevealed = false;
366:         currentStep   = -1;
367: 
368:         const sg = window.startGame || (typeof startGame !== 'undefined' ? startGame : null);
369:         if (typeof sg === 'function') {
370:             try { sg(1); } catch(e) { console.error('TutorialMode: startGame() threw:', e); }
371:         } else {
372:             console.error('TutorialMode: startGame() not found — cannot launch tutorial.');
373:         }
374:         // Give the game a moment to finish rendering before showing step 0
375:         setTimeout(setupBoard, 400);
376:     }
377: 
378:     function setupBoard() {
379:         try {
380:             // Prime earth deck so the player always draws Earth V from the earth shrine
381:             primeEarthDeck(window.spellSystem);
382:             if (typeof fitBoardToView === 'function') fitBoardToView();
383:         } catch (err) {
384:             console.error('TutorialMode: board setup error (non-fatal):', err);
385:         }
386:         // Ensure End Turn button is visible — it starts as display:none in HTML
387:         // and may have been re-hidden by lobby reset flows before the tutorial launched.
388:         const etBtn = document.getElementById('end-turn');
389:         if (etBtn) etBtn.style.display = '';
390:         showExitButton();
391:         showStep(0);
392:     }
393: 
394:     function showExitButton() {
395:         if (exitBtnEl) return;
396:         const btn = document.createElement('button');
397:         btn.className = 'tmode-exit-persistent';
398:         btn.textContent = 'Exit Tutorial';
399:         btn.addEventListener('click', finish);
400:         document.body.appendChild(btn);
401:         exitBtnEl = btn;
402:     }
403: 
404:     function hideExitButton() {
405:         if (exitBtnEl) { exitBtnEl.remove(); exitBtnEl = null; }
406:     }
407: 
408:     /** Called from game-core.js placeTile() when the local player drops their tile. */
409:     function onPlayerTilePlaced() {
410:         if (currentStep !== 1) return;   // step 1 = 'tile-placed'
411:         setTimeout(() => showStep(2), 600);
412:     }
413: 
414:     function primeEarthDeck(ss) {
415:         if (!ss?.scrollDecks?.earth) return;
416:         const deck = ss.scrollDecks.earth;
417:         const idx  = deck.indexOf('EARTH_SCROLL_5');
418:         if (idx > 0)  deck.splice(idx, 1);
419:         if (deck[0] !== 'EARTH_SCROLL_5') deck.unshift('EARTH_SCROLL_5');
420:     }
421: 
422:     // ── Spotlight system ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
423: 
424:     /**
425:      * Highlight a UI element.
426:      * blocking=true  → also add a full-screen dim overlay that eats all clicks
427:      *                  (use for action:'click' steps so only the target is interactive)
428:      * blocking=false → just glow the element; board + pawns stay fully interactive
429:      *                  (use for action:'move' and action:'place-tile' steps)
430:      */
431:     function showSpotlight(selector, blocking) {
432:         clearSpotlight();
433:         const el = selector ? document.querySelector(selector) : null;
434:         if (!el) return;
435: 
436:         if (blocking) {
437:             // The overlay sits at z-index 8600 at the document root.
438:             // If the spotlit element lives inside a positioned ancestor that has its
439:             // own stacking context (e.g. .dock-bar at z-index 50), the element's own
440:             // z-index:8620 is scoped inside that context and stays visually beneath
441:             // the overlay.  Fix: walk up the ancestor chain and temporarily lift any
442:             // stacking-context ancestor above the overlay.
443:             liftedAncestors = [];
444:             let node = el.parentElement;
445:             while (node && node !== document.body) {
446:                 const zi = window.getComputedStyle(node).zIndex;
447:                 if (zi !== 'auto' && parseInt(zi, 10) < 8650) {
448:                     liftedAncestors.push({ node, original: node.style.zIndex });
449:                     node.style.zIndex = '8650';
450:                 }
451:                 node = node.parentElement;
452:             }
453: 
454:             const ov = document.createElement('div');
455:             ov.id        = 'tutorial-blocking-overlay';
456:             ov.className = 'tutorial-blocking-overlay';
457:             ov.addEventListener('click', () => {
458:                 if (typeof updateStatus === 'function')
459:                     updateStatus('Click the highlighted element to continue the tutorial.');
460:             });
461:             document.body.appendChild(ov);
462:             overlayEl = ov;
463:         }
464: 
465:         el.classList.add('tutorial-spotlight');
466:         spotlightEl = el;
467:     }
468: 
469:     function clearSpotlight() {
470:         if (spotlightEl) {
471:             spotlightEl.classList.remove('tutorial-spotlight');
472:             spotlightEl = null;
473:         }
474:         if (overlayEl) { overlayEl.remove(); overlayEl = null; }
475: 
476:         // Restore any ancestors whose z-index was temporarily raised
477:         liftedAncestors.forEach(({ node, original }) => { node.style.zIndex = original; });
478:         liftedAncestors = [];
479: 
480:         // Remove any leftover click listener
481:         if (spotlightHandler) {
482:             spotlightHandler.el.removeEventListener('click', spotlightHandler.fn);
483:             spotlightHandler = null;
484:         }
485:     }
486: 
487:     function attachClickAdvance(selector) {
488:         const el = selector ? document.querySelector(selector) : null;
489:         if (!el) return;
490:         const fn = () => { advance(); };
491:         el.addEventListener('click', fn, { once: true });
492:         spotlightHandler = { el, fn };
493:     }
494: 
495:     // ── Board ring ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
496: 
497:     function showBoardRing(x, y) {
498:         removeBoardRing();
499:         const svg = document.getElementById('board');
500:         if (!svg) return;
501:         const vp = svg.querySelector('#viewport') || svg;
502: 
503:         const c = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'circle');
504:         c.setAttribute('cx', x);
505:         c.setAttribute('cy', y);
506:         c.setAttribute('r',  58);
507:         c.setAttribute('fill',         'none');
508:         c.setAttribute('stroke',       '#69d83a');
509:         c.setAttribute('stroke-width', '4');
510:         c.setAttribute('class',        'tutorial-highlight-ring');
511:         c.style.pointerEvents = 'none';
512:         vp.appendChild(c);
513:         boardRingEl = c;
514:     }
515: 
516:     function removeBoardRing() {
517:         if (boardRingEl) { boardRingEl.remove(); boardRingEl = null; }
518:     }
519: 
520:     // ── Modal ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
521: 
522:     function showModal(step) {
523:         closeModal();
524: 
525:         const isCorner  = step.modalPos === 'corner';
526:         const posStyle  = isCorner
527:             ? 'align-items:flex-end; justify-content:flex-end; padding:16px; background:none; pointer-events:none;'
528:             : '';
529:         const boxStyle  = isCorner
530:             ? 'pointer-events:all; max-width:380px; border:2px solid var(--accent-gold,#d9b08c);'
531:             : 'max-width:460px;';
532: 
533:         const actionHints = {
534:             'move':          'Drag your pawn to the glowing tile to continue…',
535:             'explore':       'Drag your pawn onto any face-down tile to flip it…',
536:             'click':         'Click the highlighted element to continue…',
537:             'place-tile':    'Drag your player tile onto the board to continue…',
538:             'end-turn':      'Walk to the glowing shrine center, then click End Turn…',
539:             'scroll-moved':  'Open your Hand panel and click "Move to Active Area" on the Avalanche scroll…',
540:             'stone-placed':  'Drag an Earth stone from the stone pool and drop it adjacent to your pawn…',
541:             'pattern-built': 'Build the Avalanche pattern (4 Earth stones) around your pawn — see the scroll card for the layout…',
542:             'spell-cast':    'Click "Cast Spell" in the dock (or the Cast ✦ button on the scroll card) to cast Avalanche…',
543:             'stone-broken':  'Right-click an Earth stone to break it (costs 5 AP)…',
544:             'stone-placed-wind': 'Drag a Wind stone (yellow) from the pool and place it near the trap ring…',
545:             'stone-placed-fire': 'Drag a Fire stone (red) from the pool and place it adjacent to an Earth stone…',
546:             'scripted-ai':       'Watch the opponent\'s move…',
547:         };
548:         const footerHTML = (step.nextLabel && !actionHints[step.action])
549:             ? `<button class="tmode-next">${step.nextLabel}</button>`
550:             : step.action in actionHints
551:                 ? `<span style="color:#aaa;font-size:13px;font-style:italic;">${actionHints[step.action]}</span>`
552:                 : `<span style="color:#aaa;font-size:13px;font-style:italic;">Complete the action above to continue…</span>`;
553: 
554:         const overlay = document.createElement('div');
555:         overlay.className = `tutorial-modal tutorial-tmode${isCorner ? ' tutorial-corner' : ''}`;
556:         if (isCorner) overlay.style.cssText = posStyle;
557: 
558:         overlay.innerHTML = `
559:             <div class="tutorial-content" style="${boxStyle}">
560:                 <div class="tutorial-header">
561:                     <span class="tutorial-step-label">Step ${currentStep + 1} of ${STEPS.length}</span>
562:                     <h3 class="tutorial-title">${step.title}</h3>
563:                 </div>
564:                 <div class="tutorial-body">${step.content}</div>
565:                 <div class="tutorial-footer"
566:                      style="display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;margin-top:14px;gap:8px;">
567:                     ${footerHTML}
568:                 </div>
569:             </div>`;
570: 
571:         document.body.appendChild(overlay);
572:         modalEl = overlay;
573:         // Force opacity to 1 immediately; also add the class for the CSS transition
574:         overlay.style.opacity = '1';
575:         requestAnimationFrame(() => overlay.classList.add('tutorial-visible'));
576: 
577:         const btn = overlay.querySelector('.tmode-next');
578:         if (btn) btn.addEventListener('click', advance);
579:     }
580: 
581:     function closeModal() {
582:         if (!modalEl) return;
583:         modalEl.classList.add('tutorial-fade-out');
584:         const el = modalEl;
585:         setTimeout(() => el.remove(), 220);
586:         modalEl = null;
587:     }
588: 
589:     // ── Step machine ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
590: 
591:     /**
592:      * Per-step resource grants for the trap sequence. Called from showStep BEFORE
593:      * the modal renders so the modal text is accurate when the player reads it.
594:      */
595:     function prepareStepEntry(stepId) {
596:         const ss = window.spellSystem;
597:         if (stepId === 'break-trap') {
598:             // Set AP to exactly 5 so player can afford one Earth break (cost 5).
599:             if (ss) { ss.actionPoints = 5; if (typeof updateHUD === 'function') updateHUD(); }
600:         } else if (stepId === 'opponent-retrap') {
601:             // No AP grant needed — player rests naturally to recover
602:         } else if (stepId === 'wind-escape') {
603:             if (ss?.playerPool && (ss.playerPool.wind || 0) < 1) {
604:                 ss.playerPool.wind = (ss.playerPool.wind || 0) + 2;
605:                 if (typeof updateHUD === 'function') updateHUD();
606:                 if (typeof updateStonePoolDisplay === 'function') updateStonePoolDisplay();
607:             }
608:         } else if (stepId === 'fire-counter') {
609:             if (ss?.playerPool && (ss.playerPool.fire || 0) < 1) {
610:                 ss.playerPool.fire = (ss.playerPool.fire || 0) + 2;
611:                 if (typeof updateHUD === 'function') updateHUD();
612:                 if (typeof updateStonePoolDisplay === 'function') updateStonePoolDisplay();
613:             }
614:             // Safety net: ensure at least one earth stone remains on board
615:             const hasEarth = Array.isArray(window.placedStones)
616:                 && window.placedStones.some(s => s.type === 'earth');
617:             if (!hasEarth && typeof window.placeStoneVisually === 'function' && window.playerPosition) {
618:                 // Place an earth stone east of the player at hp(3, 0) to avoid pixel-grid issues
619:                 const { x: nx, y: ny } = hp(3, 0);
620:                 window.placeStoneVisually(nx, ny, 'earth');
621:             }
622:         }
623:     }
624: 
625:     function showStep(index) {
626:         const step = STEPS[index];
627:         if (!step) return;
628:         currentStep = index;
629:         prepareStepEntry(step.id);
630: 
631:         // Clear previous decorations
632:         clearSpotlight();
633:         clearPatternPoll();
634:         clearHintTimer();
635:         removeOpponentSpeechBubble();
636:         removeBoardRing();
637:         window.tutorialAllowedHexes = null;
638: 
639:         // Board ring on move step. freeMove:true shows the ring without
640:         // restricting the pawn — used for the shrine step where the player
641:         // may need to navigate back from wherever they ended up.
642:         if (step.boardRing) {
643:             const _ringTarget = step.boardRingTarget || EARTH_POS;
644:             showBoardRing(_ringTarget.x, _ringTarget.y);
645:             if (!step.freeMove) {
646:                 window.tutorialAllowedHexes = new Set([
647:                     `${Math.round(EARTH_POS.x)},${Math.round(EARTH_POS.y)}`
648:                 ]);
649:             }
650:         }
651: 
652:         // Spotlight an HTML element.
653:         // Only block all other interaction for 'click' steps — move/place-tile steps
654:         // need the board to stay interactive so the player can drag pawns/tiles.
655:         if (step.spotlight) {
656:             const blocking = (step.action === 'click');
657:             showSpotlight(step.spotlight, blocking);
658:             if (step.action === 'click') {
659:                 attachClickAdvance(step.spotlight);
660:             }
661:         }
662: 
663:         showModal(step);
664: 
665:         // Start pattern polling for 'pattern-built' steps
666:         if (step.action === 'pattern-built') {
667:             patternPollInterval = setInterval(() => {
668:                 const ss = window.spellSystem;
669:                 if (ss && typeof ss.checkPattern === 'function' && ss.checkPattern('EARTH_SCROLL_5')) {
670:                     clearPatternPoll();
671:                     clearHintTimer();
672:                     advance();
673:                 }
674:             }, 500);
675:             startHintTimer('Build the Avalanche pattern (4 Earth stones) around your pawn — see the scroll card for the layout…');
676:         }
677:         // Start hint timers for other action-gated steps
678:         const hintMessages = {
679:             'scroll-moved':  'Open your Hand panel and click "Move to Active Area" on the Avalanche scroll…',
680:             'stone-placed':  'Drag an Earth stone from the stone pool and drop it adjacent to your pawn…',
681:             'spell-cast':    'Click "Cast Spell" in the dock after placing the pattern…',
682:             'stone-broken':  'Right-click an Earth stone to break it (costs 5 AP)…',
683:             'stone-placed-wind': 'Drag a Wind stone from the pool and drop it near the trap ring…',
684:             'stone-placed-fire': 'Drag a Fire stone from the pool adjacent to an Earth stone…',
685:         };
686:         if (hintMessages[step.action]) {
687:             startHintTimer(hintMessages[step.action]);
688:         }
689: 
690:         // Scripted AI trigger for trap steps
691:         if (step.action === 'scripted-ai') {
692:             const pos = window.playerPosition || PLAYER_POS;
693:             if (step.id === 'opponent-trap') {
694:                 showOpponentSpeechBubble('Hah! Trapped!');
695:                 setTimeout(() => {
696:                     runScriptedOpponentTrap(pos.x, pos.y, () => {
697:                         setTimeout(advance, 800);
698:                     });
699:                 }, 1200);   // delay so player sees the modal before stones drop
700:             } else if (step.id === 'opponent-retrap') {
701:                 showOpponentSpeechBubble("Can't escape twice!");
702:                 setTimeout(() => {
703:                     runScriptedOpponentRetrap(pos.x, pos.y, () => {
704:                         setTimeout(advance, 800);
705:                     });
706:                 }, 1200);
707:             }
708:         }
709:     }
710: 
711:     function advance() {
712:         const nextIdx = currentStep + 1;
713:         if (nextIdx < STEPS.length) {
714:             showStep(nextIdx);
715:         } else {
716:             finish();
717:         }
718:     }
719: 
720:     function finish() {
721:         closeModal();
722:         clearSpotlight();
723:         removeBoardRing();
724:         hideExitButton();
725:         window.isTutorialMode       = false;
726:         window.tutorialAllowedHexes = null;
727:         // Reload returns the player to the auth/lobby screen cleanly.
728:         // The tutorial dirtied the game state so there's nothing to preserve.
729:         window.location.reload();
730:     }
731: 
732:     // ── Hooks called from game code ───────────────────────────────────────────
733: 
734:     /**
735:      * Called from game-core.js BEFORE tile visuals are built.
736:      * We can override tile.shrineType here and the visual + scroll will both reflect it.
737:      */
738:     function onTilePreReveal(tile) {
739:         if (currentStep !== 3 || earthRevealed) return;
740:         // Force the first tile the player steps on to be Earth — regardless of
741:         // where they placed their player tile or which direction they moved.
742:         tile.shrineType = 'earth';
743:         primeEarthDeck(window.spellSystem);
744:     }
745: 
746:     /**
747:      * Called from game-core.js AFTER tile visuals are built and the scroll is drawn.
748:      * We advance the tutorial here so the scroll is already in the player's hand.
749:      */
750:     function onTileRevealed(tile, spellSystem) {
751:         if (currentStep !== 3 || earthRevealed) return;
752:         earthRevealed = true;
753:         setTimeout(() => showStep(4), 900); // let the flip animation finish
754:     }
755: 
756:     /** Called from game-ui.js after a successful pawn move. */
757:     function onPlayerMoved(toX, toY) {
758:         // During the shrine step, hint the player when they reach the Earth center.
759:         const SHRINE_STEP = STEPS.findIndex(s => s.id === 'earth-shrine');
760:         if (currentStep === SHRINE_STEP) {
761:             const atShrine = Math.round(toX) === Math.round(EARTH_POS.x) &&
762:                              Math.round(toY) === Math.round(EARTH_POS.y);
763:             if (atShrine && typeof updateStatus === 'function') {
764:                 updateStatus("You're at the Earth shrine center! Click End Turn to collect 5 stones.");
765:             }
766:         }
767:     }
768: 
769:     /**
770:      * Called from game-ui.js after shrine stone replenishment when the player
771:      * clicks End Turn. pos is the player's current {x, y} position.
772:      */
773:     function onEndTurn(pos) {
774:         const SHRINE_STEP = STEPS.findIndex(s => s.id === 'earth-shrine');
775:         if (currentStep !== SHRINE_STEP) return;
776:         // Use a distance check — pawn positions have floating-point pixel values,
777:         // so an exact coordinate match (===) reliably fails even when the player
778:         // is visually standing on the shrine center.
779:         const dist = pos
780:             ? Math.sqrt(Math.pow(pos.x - EARTH_POS.x, 2) + Math.pow(pos.y - EARTH_POS.y, 2))
781:             : Infinity;
782:         if (dist < 20) {
783:             // Give the stone animation a moment to play before advancing
784:             setTimeout(() => showStep(SHRINE_STEP + 1), 900);
785:         } else {
786:             if (typeof updateStatus === 'function')
787:                 updateStatus('Walk to the glowing Earth shrine center first, then click End Turn.');
788:         }
789:     }
790: 
791:     /** Called from game-ui.js when tutorial blocks an out-of-bounds drop. */
792:     function showMovementHint() {
793:         if (typeof updateStatus === 'function')
794:             updateStatus('Tutorial: drag your pawn onto a face-down tile to continue!');
795:     }
796: 
797:     function clearHintTimer() {
798:         if (hintTimer) { clearTimeout(hintTimer); hintTimer = null; }
799:     }
800: 
801:     function startHintTimer(message) {
802:         clearHintTimer();
803:         hintTimer = setTimeout(() => {
804:             if (typeof updateStatus === 'function') updateStatus(message);
805:         }, 15000);
806:     }
807: 
808:     function clearPatternPoll() {
809:         if (patternPollInterval) { clearInterval(patternPollInterval); patternPollInterval = null; }
810:     }
811: 
812:     /** Called from game-core.js after a stone is placed on the board. */
813:     function onStonePlaced(stoneType, x, y) {
814:         const step = STEPS[currentStep];
815:         if (!step) return;
816:         if (step.action === 'stone-placed' && (!step.stoneType || step.stoneType === stoneType)) {
817:             clearHintTimer();
818:             clearPatternPoll();
819:             setTimeout(advance, 400);
820:         } else if (step.action === 'stone-placed-wind' && stoneType === 'wind') {
821:             clearHintTimer();
822:             setTimeout(advance, 400);
823:         } else if (step.action === 'stone-placed-fire' && stoneType === 'fire') {
824:             clearHintTimer();
825:             setTimeout(advance, 400);
826:         }
827:     }
828: 
829:     /** Called from game-core.js after a stone is broken/removed from the board. */
830:     function onStoneBroken(stoneType, x, y) {
831:         const step = STEPS[currentStep];
832:         if (!step) return;
833:         if (step.action === 'stone-broken') {
834:             clearHintTimer();
835:             clearPatternPoll();
836:             setTimeout(advance, 400);
837:         }
838:     }
839: 
840:     /** Called from scroll-panels.js when player moves a scroll between areas. */
841:     function onScrollMoved(scrollName, fromArea, toArea) {
842:         const step = STEPS[currentStep];
843:         if (!step) return;
844:         if (step.action === 'scroll-moved') {
845:             // Gate: must be moving TO active area to satisfy this step
846:             if (toArea === 'active') {
847:                 clearHintTimer();
848:                 setTimeout(advance, 400);
849:             }
850:         }
851:     }
852: 
853:     /** Called from game-core.js when a spell is successfully cast. */
854:     function onSpellCast(scrollName) {
855:         const step = STEPS[currentStep];
856:         if (!step) return;
857:         if (step.action === 'spell-cast') {
858:             clearHintTimer();
859:             clearPatternPoll();
860:             setTimeout(advance, 600);
861:         }
862:     }
863: 
864:     // ── Scripted opponent AI ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
865: 
866:     /**
867:      * Scripted opponent trap — narrative pause, then auto-advance.
868:      * Stone placement removed: coordinate-scale mismatch (S=80 vs TILE_SIZE=20)
869:      * caused stones to land far off-board. Player's own Avalanche stones serve
870:      * as the earth stones to break in the break-trap step.
871:      */
872:     function runScriptedOpponentTrap(centerX, centerY, callback) {
873:         // Scripted stone placement removed — coordinate-scale mismatch between
874:         // tutorial's S=80 pixel grid and game's TILE_SIZE=20 grid means computed
875:         // positions land far off-board. The player's own Avalanche stones already
876:         // provide earth stones to break in the break-trap step.
877:         // Just pause briefly (opponent "thinking") then call callback.
878:         setTimeout(callback, 1200);
879:     }
880: 
881:     /**
882:      * Scripted opponent retrap — narrative pause, then auto-advance.
883:      * Stone placement removed for same reason as runScriptedOpponentTrap.
884:      */
885:     function runScriptedOpponentRetrap(centerX, centerY, callback) {
886:         // Same as runScriptedOpponentTrap — scripted stone placement removed.
887:         // Narrative text describes what the opponent "does", but no stones are
888:         // dropped visually. Player must place their own Wind stone to advance.
889:         setTimeout(callback, 1200);
890:     }
891: 
892:     /**
893:      * Show a speech bubble SVG text element above ENEMY_POS.
894:      * Removes any existing bubble first.
895:      * text: the string to display.
896:      */
897:     function showOpponentSpeechBubble(text) {
898:         removeOpponentSpeechBubble();
899:         const svg = document.getElementById('board');
900:         if (!svg) return;
901:         const vp = svg.querySelector('#viewport') || svg;
902: 
903:         const g = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'g');
904:         g.id = 'tutorial-opponent-bubble';
905:         g.setAttribute('transform', `translate(${ENEMY_POS.x}, ${ENEMY_POS.y - 55})`);
906: 
907:         const rect = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'rect');
908:         rect.setAttribute('x', '-60');
909:         rect.setAttribute('y', '-22');
910:         rect.setAttribute('width', '120');
911:         rect.setAttribute('height', '26');
912:         rect.setAttribute('rx', '6');
913:         rect.setAttribute('fill', '#2c1a3e');
914:         rect.setAttribute('stroke', '#ed1b43');
915:         rect.setAttribute('stroke-width', '1.5');
916:         rect.style.pointerEvents = 'none';
917: 
918:         const t = document.createElementNS('http://www.w3.org/2000/svg', 'text');
919:         t.setAttribute('text-anchor', 'middle');
920:         t.setAttribute('dominant-baseline', 'middle');
921:         t.setAttribute('fill', '#f3ecd8');
922:         t.setAttribute('font-size', '11');
923:         t.style.pointerEvents = 'none';
924:         t.textContent = text;
925: 
926:         g.appendChild(rect);
927:         g.appendChild(t);
928:         vp.appendChild(g);
929:     }
930: 
931:     function removeOpponentSpeechBubble() {
932:         const el = document.getElementById('tutorial-opponent-bubble');
933:         if (el) el.remove();
934:     }
935: 
936:     // ── Public API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
937:     return {
938:         start, advance, finish,
939:         onTilePreReveal, onTileRevealed, onPlayerMoved, onPlayerTilePlaced, onEndTurn, showMovementHint,
940:         onStonePlaced, onStoneBroken, onScrollMoved, onSpellCast,
941:         get currentStep() { return currentStep; }
942:     };
943: })();
944: 
945: window.TutorialMode = TutorialMode;
</file>

<file path="js/game-ui.js">

   2:         // NEW UI FUNCTIONS

   5:         function initializeNewUI() {
   6:             console.log('🎨 Initializing new UI...');
   7: 
   8:             // Setup panel toggle buttons
   9:             const toggleLeftBtn = document.getElementById('toggle-left-panel');
  10:             const toggleRightBtn = document.getElementById('toggle-right-panel');
  11:             const gameLayout = document.getElementById('game-layout');
  12: 
  13:             if (toggleLeftBtn) {
  14:                 toggleLeftBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  15:                     gameLayout.classList.toggle('left-collapsed');
  16:                     toggleLeftBtn.textContent = gameLayout.classList.contains('left-collapsed') ? '\u25B6' : '\u25C0';
  17:                 });
  18:                 toggleLeftBtn.textContent = gameLayout.classList.contains('left-collapsed') ? '\u25B6' : '\u25C0';
  19:             }
  20: 
  21:             if (toggleRightBtn) {
  22:                 toggleRightBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  23:                     gameLayout.classList.toggle('right-collapsed');
  24:                     toggleRightBtn.textContent = gameLayout.classList.contains('right-collapsed') ? '\u25C0' : '\u25B6';
  25:                 });
  26:                 toggleRightBtn.textContent = gameLayout.classList.contains('right-collapsed') ? '\u25C0' : '\u25B6';
  27:             }
  28: 
  29:             // Setup stone card drag handlers for new UI
  30:             const stoneCards = document.querySelectorAll('.stone-card');
  31:             stoneCards.forEach(card => {
  32:                 const element = card.dataset.element;
  33:                 if (element) {
  34:                     setupStoneDragFromCard(card, element);
  35:                 }
  36:             });
  37: 
  38:             // Setup scroll deck UI
  39:             initializeScrollDeckUI();
  40: 
  41:             // Common area popout: expanded = show stone patterns, minimized = names only
  42:             let commonAreaExpanded = false;
  43:             const toggleCommonAreaBtn = document.getElementById('toggle-common-area');
  44:             const commonAreaContainer = document.getElementById('common-area-container');
  45:             if (toggleCommonAreaBtn && commonAreaContainer) {
  46:                 function setCommonAreaToggleLabel() {
  47:                     toggleCommonAreaBtn.textContent = commonAreaExpanded ? '\u25C0' : '\u25B6';
  48:                     toggleCommonAreaBtn.title = commonAreaExpanded ? 'Minimize common area (hide patterns)' : 'Expand common area (show stone patterns)';
  49:                     commonAreaContainer.classList.toggle('common-area-expanded', commonAreaExpanded);
  50:                     commonAreaContainer.dataset.expanded = commonAreaExpanded ? 'true' : 'false';
  51:                 }
  52:                 toggleCommonAreaBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  53:                     commonAreaExpanded = !commonAreaExpanded;
  54:                     setCommonAreaToggleLabel();
  55:                     updateCommonAreaUI();
  56:                 });
  57:                 setCommonAreaToggleLabel();
  58:             }
  59: 
  60:             // Opponent active scrolls: show/hide stone patterns (same idea as common area popout)
  61:             let opponentPatternsExpanded = false;
  62:             const toggleOpponentPatternsBtn = document.getElementById('toggle-opponent-patterns');
  63:             const rightPanel = document.getElementById('right-panel');
  64:             if (toggleOpponentPatternsBtn && rightPanel) {
  65:                 function setOpponentPatternsToggleLabel() {
  66:                     toggleOpponentPatternsBtn.textContent = opponentPatternsExpanded ? '\u25C0' : '\u25B6';
  67:                     toggleOpponentPatternsBtn.title = opponentPatternsExpanded ? 'Hide opponent scroll patterns' : 'Show opponent active scroll patterns';
  68:                     rightPanel.dataset.opponentPatternsExpanded = opponentPatternsExpanded ? 'true' : 'false';
  69:                 }
  70:                 toggleOpponentPatternsBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  71:                     opponentPatternsExpanded = !opponentPatternsExpanded;
  72:                     setOpponentPatternsToggleLabel();
  73:                     updateOpponentPanel();
  74:                 });
  75:                 setOpponentPatternsToggleLabel();
  76:             }
  77: 
  78:             // Initial HUD update
  79:             updateHUD();
  80:         }
  81: 
  82:         // Initialize scroll deck UI with right-click handlers
  83:         function initializeScrollDeckUI() {
  84:             const deckCards = document.querySelectorAll('.scroll-deck-card');
  85:             deckCards.forEach(card => {
  86:                 const element = card.dataset.element;
  87:                 if (element) {
  88:                     // Right-click to browse deck (gated by console flag)
  89:                     card.addEventListener('contextmenu', (e) => {
  90:                         e.preventDefault();
  91:                         if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.SHOW_SCROLL_DECK_BROWSER) {
  92:                             showScrollDeckBrowser(element);
  93:                         }
  94:                     });
  95: 
  96:                     // Left-click also opens browser for convenience (gated)
  97:                     card.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
  98:                         if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.SHOW_SCROLL_DECK_BROWSER) {
  99:                             showScrollDeckBrowser(element);
 100:                         }
 101:                     });
 102:                 }
 103:             });
 104: 
 105:             // Update deck counts initially
 106:             updateScrollDeckUI();
 107:         }
 108: 
 109:         // Update scroll deck UI to show current deck counts and common area
 110:         function updateScrollDeckUI() {
 111:             if (!spellSystem) return;
 112: 
 113:             const elements = ['earth', 'water', 'fire', 'wind', 'void', 'catacomb'];
 114:             elements.forEach(element => {
 115:                 const countEl = document.getElementById(`${element}-deck-count`);
 116:                 const cardEl = document.getElementById(`${element}-scroll-deck`);
 117:                 if (countEl && spellSystem.scrollDecks) {
 118:                     const count = spellSystem.scrollDecks[element]?.length || 0;
 119:                     countEl.textContent = count;
 120: 
 121:                     // Add empty class if deck is empty
 122:                     if (cardEl) {
 123:                         cardEl.classList.toggle('empty', count === 0);
 124:                     }
 125:                 }
 126:             });
 127: 
 128:             // Update common area display
 129:             updateCommonAreaUI();
 130:         }
 131: 
 132:         // Update common area UI
 133:         function updateCommonAreaUI() {
 134:             if (!spellSystem) return;
 135: 
 136:             const container = document.getElementById('common-area-container');
 137:             if (!container) return;
 138:             if (container.dataset.expanded === undefined) container.dataset.expanded = 'false';
 139: 
 140:             const commonScrolls = spellSystem.getCommonAreaScrolls();
 141: 
 142:             if (commonScrolls.length === 0) {
 143:                 container.innerHTML = '<div class="common-area-empty">No scrolls in common area</div>';
 144:                 return;
 145:             }
 146: 
 147:             container.innerHTML = '';
 148:             commonScrolls.forEach(scrollName => {
 149:                 const scrollInfo = spellSystem.patterns?.[scrollName] || SCROLL_DEFINITIONS?.[scrollName];
 150:                 if (!scrollInfo) return;
 151: 
 152:                 const element = spellSystem.getScrollElement(scrollName);
 153:                 const scrollEl = document.createElement('div');
 154:                 scrollEl.className = `common-area-scroll ${element}`;
 155:                 scrollEl.title = scrollInfo.description || '';
 156: 
 157:                 const nameEl = document.createElement('div');
 158:                 nameEl.className = 'common-area-scroll-name';
 159:                 nameEl.textContent = scrollInfo.name || scrollName;
 160:                 scrollEl.appendChild(nameEl);
 161: 
 162:                 // Stone formation / pattern display only when common area is expanded (popout mode)
 163:                 const isExpanded = container.dataset.expanded === 'true';
 164:                 if (isExpanded && scrollInfo.patterns && typeof spellSystem.createPatternVisual === 'function') {
 165:                     const patternVisual = spellSystem.createPatternVisual(scrollInfo, element);
 166:                     patternVisual.classList?.add?.('common-area-pattern');
 167:                     patternVisual.style.marginTop = '6px';
 168:                     patternVisual.style.padding = '8px';
 169:                     if (patternVisual.style) patternVisual.style.maxWidth = '100%';
 170:                     scrollEl.appendChild(patternVisual);
 171:                 }
 172: 
 173:                 // Click to view details
 174:                 scrollEl.addEventListener('click', () => {
 175:                     showScrollInfoPopup(scrollName, scrollInfo, element);
 176:                 });
 177: 
 178:                 container.appendChild(scrollEl);
 179:             });
 180:         }
 181: 
 182:         // Shared scroll info popup — used by common area and opponent active area
 183:         function showScrollInfoPopup(scrollName, pattern, element) {
 184:             const existing = document.getElementById('scroll-info-popup-overlay');
 185:             if (existing) existing.remove();
 186: 
 187:             const elementColor = element === 'catacomb' ? '#9b59b6' : STONE_TYPES[element]?.color || '#aaa';
 188:             const elementLabel = element ? element.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + element.slice(1) : 'Unknown';
 189:             const elementImg   = STONE_TYPES[element]?.img || '';
 190:             const scrollTitle  = pattern?.name || scrollName;
 191:             const levelText    = pattern?.level ? `Level ${pattern.level}` : '';
 192:             const description  = pattern?.description || 'No description available.';
 193: 
 194:             const overlay = document.createElement('div');
 195:             overlay.id = 'scroll-info-popup-overlay';
 196:             overlay.className = 'retro-dlg-overlay';
 197:             overlay.style.zIndex = '2000';
 198:             overlay.addEventListener('click', (e) => { if (e.target === overlay) overlay.remove(); });
 199: 
 200:             const box = document.createElement('div');
 201:             box.className = 'retro-dlg-box wide';
 202:             box.style.cssText = `
 203:                 max-width: 480px;
 204:                 width: 90vw;
 205:                 padding: 0;
 206:                 overflow: hidden;
 207:                 border: 2px solid ${elementColor};
 208:                 box-shadow: 0 0 24px ${elementColor}55, 0 4px 32px #000a;
 209:             `;
 210: 
 211:             // ── Coloured header band ──────────────────────────────────
 212:             const header = document.createElement('div');
 213:             header.style.cssText = `
 214:                 background: ${elementColor}22;
 215:                 border-bottom: 2px solid ${elementColor};
 216:                 padding: 14px 18px 10px;
 217:                 display: flex;
 218:                 align-items: center;
 219:                 gap: 10px;
 220:             `;
 221:             if (elementImg) {
 222:                 const icon = document.createElement('img');
 223:                 icon.src = elementImg;
 224:                 icon.className = 'element-icon-sm';
 225:                 icon.alt = elementLabel;
 226:                 icon.style.cssText = 'width:28px;height:28px;object-fit:contain;flex-shrink:0;';
 227:                 header.appendChild(icon);
 228:             }
 229:             const headerText = document.createElement('div');
 230:             headerText.style.cssText = 'flex:1;';
 231:             const line1 = document.createElement('div');
 232:             line1.style.cssText = `font-family:var(--font-pixel);font-size:11px;color:${elementColor};letter-spacing:2px;text-transform:uppercase;`;
 233:             line1.textContent = elementLabel + (levelText ? ' · ' + levelText : '');
 234:             headerText.appendChild(line1);
 235:             const line2 = document.createElement('div');
 236:             line2.style.cssText = 'font-family:var(--font-terminal);font-size:20px;color:#e8dcc8;margin-top:3px;line-height:1.2;word-break:break-word;';
 237:             line2.textContent = scrollTitle;
 238:             headerText.appendChild(line2);
 239:             header.appendChild(headerText);
 240:             box.appendChild(header);
 241:
